review_text:
Woow, what a brilliant park. You have the historic part as well as modern pods & cabins.
Plus location makes it so relaxing.
A perfect mixture, well done.

We stayed in the tiny home, which was an amazing choice. We had hot water, a kettle, toaster, microwave, fridge, a shower toilet and sink and all facilities were clean! Such a perfect glamping experience
Loved my stay over the weekend and will definitely be back again soon. Thank you xx

We booked here for Easter, and it was the last available place on the internet. We were a bit worried after reading the reviews, but we had no other choice.

When we arrived at the campsite, we were told that our booking site number had been changed without notice, and we were given a different number.

When we got to the new site, it was too steep and full of rocks, making it impossible to set up a tent. Even the people around us said it would be hard to camp there and suggested we go back to the reception to ask for a different site.

When we went back to the reception to ask for a different site, the reception lady didn’t apologize at all and rudely said there were no other options, and if we didn’t like it, we could leave and get a refund.

My 9-year-old child who was listening to the conversation next to us was so surprised that the receptionist was so rude. YES, she was!!!

We would like to leave but all other places were already booked, and it was a 4-hour drive back home. We had no choice but to stay there.

During our stay the only advantages of this place were its location and low price.

The roads were all dirt, so whenever a car passed it kicked up dust like a construction site. The bathroom clearly needed renovation, and for three days the kitchen appeared to need care—the sponge used for washing dishes hadn’t been replaced for 3 days. And some people talk loudly until midnight.

Bright was nice to visit, but it wasn’t a place I’d want to return to Camp Crusty.
Just love coming here. Always a pleasure thanks Steve
The cabin park is beautiful.  I thoroughly enjoyed my stay here.

Awesome basic rooms for going out and riding all day!!! Warm beds, hot showers, picnic tables and a camp kitchen. We were even able to walk into town from here. Loved it. Thanks for having us Camp Crusty
Best basic rooms for heading out to the trails all day!! Warm beds, hot showers, communal kitchen and friendly staff. Walking distance to town, quite creek nearby.

Booked the most expensive room available as it promised to have shower and warmth after a long hike.

Room is cozy, no issues there.

Shower - works but water was just warm.

Bathroom door is stuck and does not close

The air coming out of the air  conditioner never heated up.

The cabin is put together poorly as it is full of holes, i.e. there is a huge gap between the toilet pipe and the floor, so you can see the ground outside.

Overall, an incredibly poor value proposition.
Such a treat coming here with the kids.

Paid for a two night stay on a powered site, in hind sight we should have booked non-powered because we didnt need power in the end. We liked the self check in, the view of the river, the location to Mystic and all the stuff happening with the Enduro Jam. The amenities bathroom needs upgrading especially the one behind the office, doors instead of a curtains for the showers would be better and a place to get undressed/dreased privately is needed. The kitchen was handy but in colder months should probably be indoors. We would definitely come back but some upgrades would make this place better complete.

We hired a power site for week, with no regrets. The facilities are immaculate clean. Location of this park is perfect, Bike track opposite and just a short 2 minute drive to town. Whether it being first time to Bright or second consider Camp Crusty, why? Dog friendly, cleanliness, location, views and quietness. Enjoyed my stay will be coming back!

We’ve been going to camp crusty for 30+ years with an onsite van. We enjoy it being away from the hustle of bright, yet a 10 min bike ride in…Samantha is great and very accomodating. The cabins and toilets are basic but clean, however fine if you’re active. The bike track and kids ride park are opposite. If you are a home body and don’t go out.. maybe stay in one of the upgraded cabins… as the standard cabin is a step up from camping.. we’ll be back again, if onsite van were an option we’d probably put one there again.
Beautiful views, fantastic location, great riding tracks and clean rooms and well maintained amenities.  Also the staff are really friendly and helpful.

Large park with many accommodation options. Two amenities blocks both of rustic style, however very clean with great hot showers.

Sites are large with powered and non powered. The park is in a bush setting with lots of birdlife.  We enjoyed watching and listening to the Gang Gangs and Kookaburras in the later afternoon.

Staff were super and were able to help us out when we needed some assistance, thankyou :-)

Directly adjacent to the Mystic Mountain Bike Track.

This will be our choice next time we are in Bright

A very nostalgic site full of Australian history.
Very clean and rustic..
Great staff
Great Place .. Facilities are good enough for the money you spend. Clean toilets-showers, recommend for families

Second time staying here and I do enjoy it however be mindful if you have a cabin close to the staff quarters and your children are playing that staff will ask them to stay away and not laugh and be kids.
Our cabin shared the same deck and wall as other tenants and every ounce of sound was amplified.
So make sure if you want a restful night that your cabin is not adjoined. (This should really be advised when booking) Toilets and showers were cleaned regularly and staff were great and keeping on top of that. We had to leave early because the beds were far too hard and needed a good nights sleep.
On rainy days, there are mud puddles everywhere, and the public facilities are from the old days.

I generally refrain from putting any negative feedback but at this particular site we really had very bad experience.
We want there doing Easters break.
Powered site was uneven(which is.mentioned itherwise) ,the water inlet pipe was broken, we were told to manage for a day and next day the site will be changed, which never happened.
Only way to contact manager is via leaving a  voicemail.
If i can say how it was different from a govt owned national parks sites, it just had hot showers and flush toilet. Rest there was nothing. Definitely never going again there.
Lovely Easter Eve weekend. Even though it was fully booked, everything went super well ! There were no complaints about noise, disturbances, or damaged amenities
Great location,  excellent service. Thankyou 👍⛺️🔥⛰️
Campsite lives up to its name but the staff there is kind especially to kids
I have booked for 2 night here. Its the worsed place I have ever stayed. Everything is worse and I regret for my booking. If you don’t want to regret never book and come here.
Facilities are too poor. Doesn’t worth the money
Great spot for easy access into the mountains!

Stayed here with my family during the Buffalo stampede trail run. Perfect location right next to the event village and Mystic mountain bike park.
Compact, neat, older style rooms with fridge, kettle, toaster and microwave. Clean shared bathrooms and bbqs (remember to byo cooking gear).
SUPER friendly and helpful staff that even found my sons kindle and posted it back to us (THANKYOU!!!!)
Great value accomodation surrounded by nature so you can enjoy the best of Brights outdoor activities.

The family enjoyed our Camping experience at your Park. Staff were very friendly and helpful. The Park has a Rustic Appeal about it.
Bright is a Great place for a holiday.
We love staying here when we come to Bright. |Super friendly and accommodating staff. Super dog friendly right at the doorstep of the mountains. Family friendly facilities. |Couldn’t recommend more!
Really awesome place to stay in Bright. I have stayed here many times and the staff are amazing and friendly. I would recommend staying here. Love that it’s dog friendly.

Clean but needs a lot of TLC.
Ok for one night.
Great spot to stay and even better people/staff! Extremely close to the start line for Buffalo Stampede.
Shocking pruces and its like staying in a abomdon shack. WOULD NOT RECOMEND

Have stayed many times, short & long stays, this place is great, close to town, not overcrowded unlike the other parks in town so you always have some privacy when staying on either powered or unpowered sites. Unless it's peak season.. Rooms are ok, all toilets, showers and kitchen are always clean, great bunch at the reception 👌 I will always be coming back to this place and a bonus - Flo's coffee van right at the entrance, it can't get any better.

Stayed in a Heritage Hut - Twin which was very clean, comfortable and reasonably priced for the standard of accomodation. The addition of the covered decks with table and chairs at the front of the huts since the last time made for an even more enjoyable stay.

The cabin was small but very clean and with a comfortable bed.  Only disadvantage was one person having to crawl over the other to go to the toilet.  Shower and toilet facilities also very clean.  Room and facilities very old as expected but overall good value for the money compared to other offerings in Bright.  We enjoyed our one night stay there.

Glen and Rhonda kept the site clean and tidy, and were always available to help travelers needing technical advice. The camp is ideally located at the base of the bike trails. It was quiet and had lovely views.
Stayed in queen room, very comfortable. Super friendly community and well maintained services/amenities for the kitchen and bathrooms! Will definitely come back:)
Great park and everybody friendly, plan layout of park not very good but everybody managed to find  a site.

We had a wonderful three days at camp Crusty, Glen and Rhonda were fantastic hosts, taking the time to walk around and introduce themselves to everyone. The camp ground, amenities and camp kitchen were the cleanest we have ever seen. We had an amazing time and will definitely be back.

Perfect location for Brighter days
Amenities clean
No hassle 🫶

This is our third year in a row staying here. We absolutely love it here, location is prime - across the road from where the Brighter Days festival is.
We camp in our caravan, so not sure what the accommodation is like, but we re book every year 😊

We stayed for 11 wonderful days in late January 2026 and had such a fantastic experience. When we first arrived there was a small mix-up with our booking, but the manager handled it immediately and upgraded us without hesitation, which we really appreciated. The team went above and beyond to make sure we had everything we needed, and they also made sure the rest of our family was well taken care of when they arrived mid-week.

The amenities were spotless and clearly very well maintained, being cleaned twice a day. We even saw staff thoroughly cleaning a cabin from top to bottom after guests left, working hard in 30+ degree heat, which really showed their dedication.

All the staff were incredibly friendly, welcoming and always smiling. They happily shared local tips and suggestions for places to visit and were always up for a chat, which made the stay feel even more personal.

Overall it was such a relaxed, stress-free stay. The campsite was spacious and well looked after, and we thoroughly enjoyed our time there. We will definitely be back!
Rebecca & Anthony

My family have just spent the weekend at Crusty and loved it, the manager Sam was so friendly and really made the experience great by going out of her way to help us explore Crusty and Bright with the kids. It’s an affordable, simple experience and the friendly staff really made it memorable. Will be coming back next year!
So great! In all the ways. Really clean, great spot, dog friendly, easy to access all the things the high country has to offer! our new go to. Thanks heaps!

Absolutely fantastic service, thank you Michelle and the team for going out of your way to make our stay great, the tree lined sites were our favourite, site 69 in particular. Very close proximity to Bright and places to swim.

We had a wonderful stay at Camp Crusty Bright and truly enjoyed every moment. The camp is beautiful, well maintained, and very family friendly. Our cabins were allocated close together, which made things much easier with kids, and we really appreciated the thoughtful service.
The staff were friendly, helpful, and great with communication from booking through to after our stay.
Overall, it was a relaxing and enjoyable experience in a lovely setting. We’re already looking forward to visiting Camp Crusty again. Highly recommended
Thank you camp crusty team
Spoke to Michael, great gentlemen and good facilities at camp crusty 👍🏾👍🏾
Great place
Great spot down by the river great amenities dog friendly !!!
Michelle is awesome
Site was great and the people were very helpful
Great site and even better customer service
Fantastic spot with an unreal view! Well kept facilities. Happy family 👍🏻
Loved our time here; rustic facilities, however, much nicer to be enjoying the surrounding nature than being right in town squashed like sardines. Would go again!
Have stayed a few time now, located on the edge of town, quiet location, with a bush feel

A wonderful, rustic campsite at the foot of the mountains. Room to move, excellent position for biking and outdoor pursuits. Fabulous staff who do their best to accommodate you. Every tourist town needs a  'Crusty Camp'

If you love watered lawns and imaculate pools, dont come hear.
But if you want beautifull views, huge areas for kids to play, very close to mountain bike track, with vans to take you to the top and flat cycle paths Crusty is the place to stay And very well prices.
We lived it.

We stayed overnight, came in around 5ish. The creek is a little shallow at this time, but I saw crayfish so it's healthy. No fish unfortunately. If you are serous about fish, then waders and patience. Super light gear.  The facilities are a little dated, but sufficient and clean. This has the most Zen feel to it. You should come. Bright is a postcard little town.

Awesome location if you're mountain biking. Good price.
Our caravan was too big for the site they allocated, the lady gave us a new site straight away.
Great spot close to town and the river

4 days camping and Mountain Bike Riding here. 2 x Adults and 2 x Kids. The best location in Bright with easy access to Mystic and all the MTB tracks, and the river to cool down when it’s hot.

Staff are super friendly, and the amenities (camp kitchen, bathrooms, etc) were the cleanest I have seen.

Special mention to the communal BBQ’s available, which have to be the cleanest and best functioning I have ever seen.

Will certainly be coming back. 👌

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Top notch for our 3 day MTB ride. 100% be back again throughout the year. Can't beat it

A great place to stay, we camped at camp crusty at the beginning of a heatwave (40degs), the owner / manager was so helpful in placing us in a more shaded area and alongside some friends who had also booked without additional costs.

Facilities as good, they’re basic but clean and well equipped.

It’s the best location if you’re mountain biking at mystic mountain!

Thank you - always a good stay!

Great spot! At the beautiful foothills of the mountains, close to the creek, amoungst the trees and birds, how's the serenity! |If your into mountain bike riding and REAL camping, this is the spot! |The staff were very welcoming and friendly, and if you run out of pegs, Rhonda will not hesitate to lend you her own. Definitely come back! 😀👍

Absolute winner for a family MTB trip!
Camp Crusty is perfectly set up for mountain biking - literally wake up and you’re ready to ride. Can’t beat that convenience.
We stayed in the heritage miners huts which are definitely rustic, but they’ve got real charm and were clean, dry and had comfortable beds - everything you need after a big day on the trails.
There’s a gorgeous creek just a quick walk away which was perfect for the kids to jump in and cool off. Plus it’s only a short drive into Bright to hit up the river splash park, so plenty of options to keep everyone happy.
The staff were legends when we needed to rock up a day early - super helpful and made it all work without any fuss.
Can’t recommend this place enough if you’re after an active family holiday in Bright!

Had the best time here. Staff are so helpful and even upgraded our campsite spot to a shaded area to accommodate with the heatwave. Loved the location and how close it is to the river. Will definitely be coming back

The Bins stink and if you're room or camp are near its almost unbearable, the rooms are basic, no cups or plates etc just a toaster fridge and microwave. This is the worst cabin and caravan park that I've stayed in Australia. You have to go to Vietnam and pay $10 a night to get similar standards. All facilities are very poorly cared for and run down.

No staff present, no rules which was great like the 1970's or something. The location is great if you want to get away from all the hustle of Bright town area in peak periods. Proximity to the river, park and bike tracks is awesome.

It's not worth the money, camping might be a better option as it would be conditions in a tent.
Very helpful and friendly staff in a great location
Love this park. Service is always great.Amenities always clean. Owners cant do enough to make your stay great.  Its our go to always when we stay in Bright. 5 stars 🌟

Great campground. Perfect for an adventure holiday for kids.
Amenities clean and great water pressure on the showers.
Highly recommend.

Great tiny house had everything we needed. Having our own shower and toilet was great. Made it our own little place. We were there for 5 nights great price.
We were right near the River great place to cool off. Only 30 mins walk into town and did that nearly everyday.

Coffee van out front in the morning awesome. Even though we had a microwave in the Tiny house we used the BBQ’s provided for everyone. Great facilities for kitchen use.

Was just a great stay the verandah on the tiny house was fantastic sat out there all the time.

Location was great - swimmable creek on grounds, lots of beautiful tall gum trees, and a short drive from the centre of Bright. Grounds were very busy and large though - so felt a bit steep to be paying 70 bucks a night and be so close to so many other tents and the road (meant it was quite loud at night/early morning). Bathrooms were a bit ‘crusty’ but shower water was warm so can’t really complain.

Micheal really helped us out finding a good spot when it was very busy and hot. He really went beyond good service. The creek was beautiful and it is right next to the bike park so you can get in heaps of laps.
Yes!
Awesome campsite!!
Amazing staff, amazing location. Family and dog friendly.. everything you need. We will be back.

My gosh there are words that cannot describe this place without swearing. I’m not sure where the positive reviews come from maybe they are paid for. This is without the worst place you could imagine. We booked in for 2 nights but left soon after arrival and we were blessed that NRMA Bright caravan park had a site available which is great. Crusty's  is a disgrace. Steer well clear. Do not believe the positive reviews. Look at the one stars they are a true reflection of what you get!
One of the best caravan and camp parks. Recommend it for families and people who a lot of people.
Affordable price and best place for camping with friendly environment and best staff to look after you

Excellent customer service, picturesque sites, clean facilities, one of the best places we have stayed while in Bright. Amazing value for money, close to bike trails, paragliding, 4x4 tracks and the river, a short walk to bright township and full mobile and tv reception! Bush camp feels with caravan park conveniences. Thanks Michael and the team at Camp Crusty Bright!!
Lovely man on reception. Was very helpful and super friendly. Location is perfect for mystic park. Facilities are good. Would recommend staying here.
Love this place

We arrived, found our site, got onto google, booked another caravan park and left. We were there 10 minutes. The place was dirty; a dust bowl every time the wind blew.  Our site looked out over the over flowing bins. At $120 pn, this place was a disgrace.

We booked 3 nights here and will almost certainly not see out our reservation. Dilapidated buildings with maintenance desperately needed. No one on site to enforce anything. Leave things better than you found them? Rubbish bins are overflowing and people have thrown cans throughout the grounds. We might as well be camping at a night club with the music pulsing and yelling coming from every corner well into the night. This is a gorgeous location that is being neglected in more ways than one.
Good location not right in the hustle hustle but close enough to town. Great camp sites

Camp Crusty was an amazing experience. My friends and I all stayed in cabin comfortably with no issues at all. Clean facilities for all of us to use. Also a great location close to the town and all the other activities we wanted to do. Would definitely return in the future.

Stayed here with my mates over the weekend and had a great time. The location is perfect along with the beautiful surroundings. The toilets and showers were nice. Had a bbq we could use and overall couldn’t ask anything more.

I had a really great time at Camp Crusty. I initially had an issue setting up my roof top tent due to the zip snagging, and a lovely employee (Rhonda, I think) came and helped me fix the issue. I also found the amenities to be lovely and very clean, it was so nice to have a warm shower and use the camp kitchen too.
Bright camping and caravan excellent staff all ways there to help and very friendly great location.

Perfect place for camping mountain bike and road riders to stay, we had a great spot under a shady tree in the heat. Customer sevrice was excellent, quickly handled a turn up on the day booking with the online system, very responsive.

Old, dirty, unkept is a good description. On a plus side it is close to Bright but a 20minute walk to town shops and cafes. It is a standard cost for camping so you would expect positives but considering the conditions it is overpriced.

The place reassembles a building site with mud pits and rubbish everywhere and delapidated rusty tin sheds waiting to be demolished. There was even a digger in the grounds and sections of ground dug.

Paths were dirt roads and the grassed areas uneven rough and poor condition. Site locations are poorly marked with hand marked plaques in the grass some broken or covered in dirt/mud making them difficult to find. Sites are allocated but no site layout maps are provided to find your site.

The cabins at one end are rusty tin sheds resembling those from old mine sites. Upon arrival, the owners approached me and rather rudely asked if I had a booking. This is despite them sending messages there was no need to check in. They came across as having minimalistic approach.

My allocated camp spot was in one corner whilst the two amenities blocks were together both at the opposite end of the site. The amenities were old dirty tin sheds with delapidated old fixtures in desperate need of upgrade. In winter these would be unpleasant to use. They looked dirty and smelled of stale urine which is disgusting.
I reluctantly used the facilities to wash and then quickly left. I came out of the shower block feeling more dirty than I went in. I am a person who likes rough camping but overall, the place felt dirty and uncomfortable and not a place I would stay.

I had booked a camping spot, but upon arriving there I used the amenites to wash but didn't want to  stay. The place reassembles a building site with rubbish everywhere and delapidated rusty tin sheds waiting to be demolished. There was even a digger in the grounds and sections of ground dug up. Paths were dirt roads and the grassed areas uneven rough and poor condition. The cabins at one end are rusty tin sheds resembling ones from old mine sites. The owners approached me and rather rudely asked if I had a booking. This is despite them sending me messages I dont need to check in. They came across as having minimalistic approach. My allocated camp spot was in one corner whilst the two amenities blocks were together both at the opposite end of the site. The amenities were old dirty tin sheds with delapidated old fixtures in desperate need of upgrade. In winter these would be unpleasant to use.|I reluctantly used the facilities to wash and then quickly left as the place felt uncomfortable and not a place I would stay.
Don go there if it’s raining they have no drainage what so ever

We stayed in a Tiny Home for three nights as these were the only cabins with bathroom facilities, albeit very “Tiny”
The TH was clean and modern, just too tiny for a 3 night stay for an “older” couple

Went in March 2025, drive way was terrible to drive on 8 months later the exact same. Very overpriced, rooms are a disgrace. No staff anywhere to be seen, all online which isn’t very interactive and my only interaction with them wasn’t very nice. Could have so much potential.
The park areas for the caravans is great. Excellent size sites. Unfortunately there is no dump point and the showers blocks leave a lot to be desired.

We had a great time staying at Camp Crusty. We booked two twin huts which were next to each other and under the same roof line. The deck is awesome. There was a long bench seat plus 2 chairs and a small round table outside (bring extra camp chairs if you need them). Rooms were clean. Linen fresh. Good shelving. No fans, but I could bring my own or ask for some if needed. It wasn't hot at night, so we didn't need any. Heaters in rooms. No fridge or appliances in our rooms which had single beds, but there's a camp fridge and freezer across at the camp kitchen. Padlocks on the doors work fine. Friends who had family huts did have fridge/appliances.
We loved our stay. Tanya & team were awesome. Bathroom and showers were clean and I never had to queue for either of them. Laundry and big clothes line. River a stones throw away to cool off and plenty to do around Bright and beyond. We will definitely be back. Our accommodation was absolutely fine. Anyone who is disappointed should look for a different type of experience. We couldn't fault the place. If you're staying with friends-book cabins together so you can be near each other. Night time was so quiet! I think we were the noisiest bunch chatting with friends and eating dinner on our deck.Thanks, Camp Crusty. We'll be back!
good views but it’s not that clean I found some toe nails right next to were I’m sleeping…

Friendly and service orientated management. Love the fact its a little out of town but close enough. Amenities are a bit tired and could do with some T&C but the park is loaded with charm and character. Will definitely book here again

Never seen a more accurate caravan park title than camp crusty. They keep calling it rustic, I call it dilapidated. Rustic doesn’t generally mean spider webs throughout the cabin, dog hair on the (I’m hoping) washed linen, door handles falling out, the smoke alarm hanging off, large gaps at bottoms of doors, holes in the window mesh… I wish I read reviews before I booked but I guess that’s what you get booking last minute with no availability in town. No landscaped pathways or even roads, when it rains it turns into a swamp. Very little lighting which is a safety hazard. Wish I turned straight back around but couldn’t give up the $170 a night for asbestos rooms and spider webs. Very overpriced if that wasn’t clear. Camp crusty is definitely living up to its name. It’s a shame, could be a really nice “rustic” park but they seem to have a different definition of rustic.

Oh and there’s only 2 toilet blocks for the entire park, couldn’t imagine what it’s like if this park gets at all busier.
We took our caravan and what a beautiful place to stay make sure your warm it's cold but absolutely beautiful

Certainly felt the the crust some might even say we bathed in the crust. Kinda strange to have a door that could be locked from the inside with no ability to get out from the inside. Showers were good, communal cooking facilities were more than expected. Oil heater and electric blankets were a nice addition. If you are going to Hotham this is a good affordable place to stay.

Great value for money! Beds were comfortable and the room was cosy. Showers were warm. Only problem was the septic tank was really smelly and we could smell it the whole time including while in bed! Would love for that to be fixed.

Loved the location of Camp Crusty,  can’t fault it. Beautiful setting and a great base to explore Bright. My friend stayed in one of the tiny homes and it was awesome — modern, clean, and well-designed. Honestly, those are the only rooms I’d recommend.

We stayed in a queen room, and for the price, it was pretty disappointing. The room are like a padlocked storage units. It wasn’t clean, we ended up buying our own sheet because the bedding didn’t look fresh. Definitely needs a serious update.

What really let things down was the firepit situation. One of the main reasons we booked was to sit around a fire with friends, but we had no idea reception is only open from 9am to 10am. So good luck getting a firepit and wood sorted if you arrive later in the day. That kind of info should be made clearer or even better, make it possible to book firepits and wood online in advance so it’s ready when you get there.

I’d stay again, but only in a tiny home. If I were running Camp Crusty, I’d renovate the rooms and make the fire experience way more accessible for guests.

For location and beautiful surroundings, this place has it all, it’s stunning.  If mountain trail bike riding is your thing, across the road is Mystic Park, it’s a biker’s heaven. That’s the good part.  However, Camp Crumbling as we dubbed it, falls down everywhere else.  It’s old, run down and the bathroom amenities are disgusting plus no heating. The office was empty and there doesn’t seem to be any staff around..  There are no sealed paths so make sure you have boots if you’re there during any wet weather, it’s a muddy quagmire and very poor lighting on the grounds after dark.  This place has so much potential.  Let’s hope someone takes it on and gives it some much needed TLC.
Value for money …with great view

One of the lady there,might be the manager or with the management team is not welcoming and rude, we were asking questions about the stay and she was like why do you need to know that like what?? Very uncomfortable and we regret that we asked, good luck in here

The staff was kinda mean but it is a okish place to stay
Very tricky to manage with picky kids
Solid place to stay for a night if no other accomodation is available
Btw this is for the rooms not cabins👍
🫥😉
Not bad for me and my mate, we come for snowboarding at Mt Hotham, just looking for a place to sleep, easy access, close to Bright town for groceries and food.

We had the most horrible camping experience here. We were supposed to stay 4 days at their powered camp site in tents. It was fine on our firsr night because we were prepared for the rain and the cold, we had heaters to keep us warm. But then, on our 2nd night, there was no power. We were miserable in the cold and wet. We saw that the cabins had power restored but not at our site. By morning, most of the campers in powered site, including the caravans, have packed up and left. It totally ruined our holiday.
Aside from the power fiasco, the facilities are very old. The only good thing about this camp site is the location.
Definitely not coming back.

**EDITED to add a star, and to comment that management did end up giving us a 50% refund a week later.  They assure us they are working with their energy provider to solve the supply issue. **

I want to leave a good review as we like to support basic and affordable accommodation options, but we were pretty disappointed overall.  The accommodation we stayed in (heritage huts) are old and not very weatherproof, although showers were hot and beds comfortable.

In the first instance, they made an error and did not book us a second hut adjacent to ours for our children, despite emailing us to say that this had been done.  The kids were then put in cabins a 50 metre walk away, across uneven and muddy terrain.  This definitely made our family's weekend less enjoyable.  Mistakes happen, but no apology or explanation was offered.

The power then went out in the early evening, and stayed off for most of the night.  No lights, cooking facilities, heaters, electric blankets etc were able to be used, making it difficult to stay warm (gaps under the doors and lack of insulation contributing), or enjoy our evening on our weekend away.  We may as well have been camping (without warm food, cups of tea and even torches that is), at a cost of $270!!

Again, no apology or explanation was received from the management.  Electrical problems happen, but some communication and compensation is expected in the hospitality industry - after all, the accommodation was paid for on the understanding that it included power!  Yet management declined to compensate us for a very uncomfortable night in Bright.

Camp Crusty has potential given its location, but the grounds, buildings and customer service definitely need to be improved.

We were there over Autumn. The proximity to Bright is great. Not too close, not too far. It's a short bike trip in if you prefer not to drive. The facilities are basic and old but everything is well maintained and the showers are hot. Coin operated laundry, microwave stove and fridges available but the washing up sink tends to have a queue if you don't time it right. If your plan was to mountain bike, it's a dream. Free trails, jumps and pump tracks across the road accessible via a bridge underpass so no worries about kids getting there themselves. Mystics is right at one side of the park. Od foresters cabins were very basic and you'd want to bring a fan in warmer seasons. Our family camped. The water and electricity arrangements were a bit bizarre but we got used to it. Some sites are not that square or flat. Very dusty when we were there. Keep your windows closed.
I'd give this place a five (5 stars) for what they have versus what they provide but for the fact that a couple of nights, we had inconsiderate young people yahooing well into the night and it's not so nice when you have little children. They should really enforce a curfew.
Saw some new cabins crop up but we didn't stay in those so can't comment.
Another bizarre-o thing was - On my way out, I took some photos of the kitchen facilities, then I was interrogated by a Kiwi lady staff about why I was photographing. I told her my wife, who was not present on this trip was a chef, and always interested to know what facilities are available at parks she is likely to visit (as most of these things are not always not he website) That way she can maximise the use should we return together. What kind of food and cooking can be cooked, how to prepare etc. The staff went on to lecture me about how if she's a professional, she should be able to adapt regardless etc etc. Having been Sous and Head Chef of various luxury hotels and restaurant in Sydney like, Quay Grand, Observatory/Langham, Sebel (all of them including the Townhouse) for over 30 years, I think we might have an idea how we like to roll. It was awkward and condescending. Not the best send off.
Otherwise perfect and we'll probably return next Autumn.
Fire allowed in fire pit. Plenty of fire wood you can buy here.

The Camp Crusty team have been a pleasure to deal with and super accommodating with our requests. We chose this campground due to its proximity to the Mtn Biking trails. We can’t wait for our first stay.

Booked cabin 12 MONTHS in advance for a stay in May 2025 and booked in person. Owners insist on full payment for booking (NOT deposit and balance paid closer to  arrival date) and utterly refused to accept cash, yet I was charged extra fee for using a debit card NOT a credit card. |Checked their website in Dec 2024 and a week before my arrival, both times exact cabin I had booked and same number of nights was advertised at $40 cheaper! Checked again day after I left and advertised at $20 cheaper. Does not pay to book early with these people.|Since found other cabins and their facilities in Bright area, have been updated and refurbished for the same price or small insignificant increase in price. Apart from maybe a lick of paint, this caravan park's cabins are the same as the day they were built, question whether structure still contains asbestos.
Booked a week before my stay last year, but they got the dates wrong. When I asked them to fix it, they refused. Poor service and zero accountability.
Not fancy but hot showers and clean toilets. The managers were super friendly. Great location and close to bright and bike tracks

The maps provided was out of date and showed our cabin in a placve where no such cabin exists. We had to drive around ;looking for the right one.
tioerh than that, it was serviceable!
This place is awesome if you come in for a ride just for camping 🏕️ Accommodation is Very basic, but you get what you pay for. Overall great price right underneath the Mountain I plan to come again

Crusty by name crusty by nature.
Very very tired, not rustic, rusted. Maybe my expectations were too high. Ablutions block was kind of clean, the poo my wife saw on the toilet wall might have been fresh and missed by the cleaners the next day. Are there cleaners? Never saw any management, at most parks someone will make themselves known to you.
The showers were hot and good pressure, the water from one shower flows into the next.
Sites were dusty, I do realise we are in a drought. We had an open fire in our fire pit, previous users of the sight left there coals and ash on the ground, grubs.
We have stayed in some lovely and some rustic and quirky caravan parks, this isn't one of them. Sorry.
I did notice some new constructions happening so the situation may improve.
2 stars.
Great location. Amenities are a bit tired but the price is right. Would love some more dog friendly options. Some of the new tiny homes perhaps.

Great location as its only about 200 meters from the landing site where I go paragliding. Being the owner of a mobile home all I require is access to power and water . The sites are spaced well apart , are level and most important quite. The other important feature for me being a retiree is that the tariff is very reasonable.
Highly recommend the park . I have stayed twice.

Crusty by name crusty by nature … don’t go it’s disgusting , heavy mould on cabins very back locks on doors you can lock in from the outside re: gate bolt on outside of door not to mention a half repaired hole where someone’s foot had gone through …run no stars could possibly be given … receptionist very rude
Rough and ready, but strangely fun, showers hot ,it kinda grew on us.

Spent a week there during Easter. Wasn't sure what to expect after some of the reviews but we had a lovely a stay camping on a powered site. Amenities were fine and looks like there upgrading. Would definitely stay here again.

If you cannot get one of the self contained huts it is just a step up from camping.
If you book through bookings.com chances are that will not get what you are expecting.

My sister and I had a lovely stay in your spacious caravan park. I loved the charm of the old buildings. Showers and toilets were old but clean. My only suggestion would be to have a disabled shower with a bench or seat for seniors that need when dressing after shower. Don't spoil the ambience of the park by putting new buildings maybe just up date the interiors. We will be back. 😀😄🫠😉

The tiny house was perfect. Had everything we needed and lovely views from the bed. Will be staying again when we visit Bright.
My only suggestion would be to have fly screens on the kitchen window as it got very stuffy at night unless you had the aircon on l night and I would have preferred fresh air.

Running out of hot water for shower in one of the toilet on the night i was staying , toilet is dirty and floor is always wet , when I was brushing my teeth there was a man who claimed himself was the staff just came in to the lady toilet and checked for the toilet papers while there were still people there showering. Wouldn’t complain much about the kitchen considering the price but overall you get what you paid for

We give Crusty's 10/10.  The amenities are always spotless.  There greatest asset is the Managers and staff - we had made an error on our dates and they were more than happy to assist us with our accommodation, we had a lovely stay.  We forgot a dog lead and they gave us one.  Lovely people.

太差了。要卫生没有卫生。要环境没有也没有。最糟糕是小老鼠很多。睡不着觉.被子也很臭。太差了.太差了。厕所都没有清理垃圾卫生。臭气熏天。
是见过最糟糕露营地

Great camp.
Short walk to town.
Dogs allowed
Excellent MTB trails.

Amazing location, beautiful staff and a place that will make you appreciate nature again that is not priced five star. Camp crusty has everything you need for a simple and relaxed holiday. Clean rooms and amenities and surroundings that you never grow tired of looking at. We have stayed in both the cheaper and more expensive cabins and have been really, really satisfied every time.

The owner lady is very rude and me despite paying a $100 cabin for 6 nights received a disrespectful reply  when I asked where a microwave was. I had a friend visiting for lunch time and I wanted to heat up pies for them. The owner lady said microwave is for camp tenants only. I was like I am allowed to use microwave a couple of times of I pay $600 for 6 days. It was petty and not a nice customer service skill job. The cabin was a double bed in a tiny cabin and had minimal space for $100.
Then the customers that turn up are also feral. One customer argued with me about my car being parked right in front of my own cabin. I didn't engage but the clientele and owner are both feral. So only come if you cannot find accomodation anywhere else. But then be ready for rude owner and feral customers.
Also place is very DUSTY. Dust will be everywhere even in your rooms as room doors have a gap at bottom.
The rooms are not sound proof I could hear people snoring next door and even conversations.
Three mosquito's entered on first night that I killed at 2am.

This is not a budget accommodation, it's a totally rundown place.  Even animals shelter have better amenities, cabins are in a terrible state. Do not stay here, go bush camping instead you will feel more comfortable. Owner who is also the cleaner is very rude.

We had a very unpleasant experience at Camp Crusty in Bright. The contact number listed on their Booking.com profile is just a voice recording with no option to leave a voicemail. The onsite manager, Randy, was extremely rude. Even though the checkout time listed was between 10:00 to 10:30 AM, we had already checked out by 10:00 and were simply having breakfast outside in the common area. Without any notice, she started using a blower near our kids, kicking up dust. When we politely raised concerns, she began using rude language and accused us of leaving rubbish, which was untrue. She then called another man who showed up and began swearing and threatening to call the police, behaving very aggressively. This was an extremely disappointing and stressful experience—definitely not family-friendly or professional.
I stayed in one of the cabins and have come home covered in flea bites!

We stayed in one of the Tiny house, wonderful experience! Next to Mystic Bike Park, five minutes drive to town of Bright with amazing view to Morses Creek and friendly staff. I will definitely come back.
Owner needs customer service lesson. This should be renamed Rusty camp given how deteriorated rooms/cabins and facilities are. Bush camping will be more comfort.

We just had the best time at Camp Crusty. The facilities are clean and it’s so great being nice and close to all the mountain bike trails. The staff are very nice and happy to help you with anything. We have already booked our next stay. Thanks again Camp crusty!

Avoid. Creepy site…No staff around at 4pm, no calls answered… They call it rustic, I call it a dump. No surprise that this was the only place that had spots free when everywhere else was booked out. We ended up finding a free camp at ah youngs even during busy period in case anyone else needs to escape from his hole.

Stayed here at Camp Crusty with my family for a long weekend away and it was one of the best getaways we have done. Such a great location and very friendly staff. We will definitely be back. Our son didnt want to leave, must say neither did my wife & I.

Do not stay here. The camping might be ok but the rooms were the worst i have ever stayed in. The bedding was dirty. The floors were dirty. I could have almost joined in on the conversation next door. Cockroaches everywhere, one crawled on me in the shower. PTSD from that joint.

our tiny house was great but very dusty all around -needed a broom to keep deck clean & stop dust coming in …a few hooks to hang hats/keys/coats would be handy ..

Our sons room was pretty terrible for the money we paid ,rustic is an understatement..

Pros:location. The place is 7 min bike ride to Bright. The river is next to it and it is surrounded by lovely bike rides and parks. The cabins are equipped with the essentials.

Con: pretty basic facilities. We were there during the Easter weekend and they do not have big enough bathroom facilities for the volume of people that came in. While the cleaners tried their hardest, restrooms were always dirty, place ran out of water during the first morning we were there, so we couldn’t use the toilets.
We also rented a cabin. And we weren’t expecting luxury, but it is Bright, known for being cold and we only got a comforter in the bed. No sheets, no warm blanket, only a very old, small heater to keep the place semi warm. We were so cold at night, we slept with our hoodies, pants and socks on. Definitely not worth the money we paid for the place.

Stayed here over Easter in an unpowered campsite. Sites were the smallest we have been in for some time, and so close to Neighbours (noisy). With the amount of people staying more amenities are required due to frequent queues during peak times. Other than that the proximity of the park to Mystic and Bright was excellent.

Really great experience at this camp. Staff were great and lots of help. Beautiful views of the mountains and 2 bathrooms to choose from with showers and toilets. I really recommend going here with friends and family you wont regret it.
Lots of people come here as it is really good and there is a town near by with loads of festive stores and brings good vibes. This place gives country vibes.
Entry to park is dréadful  , full of potholes. Our site 110 was very bumpy. Long grass not mowed. You are left to find yoú own site.

Beautiful experience, it’s unfortunate other campers had a terrible trip but that doesn’t make this place terrible. The staff are wonderful, you’re able to have full access to power, water, many toilets and showers also they get cleaned regularly. To add on the views are spectacular. Shops are nearby followed with bike trails to go on rides, a river and water park nearby aswell to enjoy with friends and family. This place deserves good reviews as it’s a good place for a good experience ☺️.
Amazing place with everything you need. Powered/non powered sites, cabins, camp kitchen, all amenities. Staff are amazing. We’ll definitely be back. Thanks for having us!

Wonderful place to camp with power, water, bathroom facilities. Clean showers and toilets. Hire fire pits and purchurse fire wood as well as ice. Ameneties cleaned multiple times. Staff and management are amazing and very accomodating. We come here multiple times a year.

What an amazing place. Such wonderful staff.
Thank you Camp Crusty Team we loved staying with you.

The facilities are very few, old and awful
No dump point and people flush it down the toilet
I would stay home rather than stay here ever again

Well, I have stayed in many caravan parks cabins, hotel rooms and low budget accommodation, this one in my view and 4 other friends (5 rooms total) was  the lowest most dilapidated, run down and  very very uncomfortable  place I have stayed in. It cost  $110.00 each, if I was charged 1/2 that much it would have still been  too much for what was on offer and NO I would not have paid even $50.00 after I saw the place. It might have compared to a condemned dwelling only worse. the bed was dirty, could not get around it as was pushed into a corner or else the door could not be opened, the door had a pad bolt on the inside but also 1 on the outside therefore someone could have locked you in. the amenities were very basic and only the men's was open, women had to share showers and my wife was in a partial undress when men came in. Thanks for the poor attempt at a joke and ripping people off, NEVER AGAIN

Lovely laid back campground. The amenities are basic but clean- and the showers always had hot water. Laundry facilities: nice new machines $5 per load. Close to mystic bike park and not far from town

We love this place. We are booked for 3 nights in a hut and quite happy after night 1, bed is comfortable, room stays quite warm with the heat on lowest setting overnight, it's a happy little environment and we'll definitely be back

A wonderful camp, 2 time here. Location perfect and the staff so accomodating . A massive thank you to Damien for his assistance for a weld repair on our caravan spare wheel holders. Nothing a bother. We definitely be back. Thank you for an enjoyable stay.

This place is the best! The location for us is perfect. Every time we come which is 10+ times BTW, we are always wrapped to be here. Walking tracks close by and Mystic shuttles are literally 10sec away. We will continue to stay here!

Would’ve been great if the tiny houses were dog friendly.
The Heritage houses were past their use by date.

POOR CUSTOMER SERVICE THEY STOLE MY MONEY.
Absolutely Poor customer service , told them 9 days before my booking that ive been in an accident with broken vertebrae and need to cancel, they then said you can only reschedule i said id prefer a refund but that will have to do, now because i didnt send my request in a separate email they didnt accept my request and kept my money and canceled my booking, discusting customer service.

Camp Crusty is the perfect location if your up for an adventure.  It's located right next to Mystic Bike Park which is so convenient! We had a great camp site after Management were very helpful and accommodating in offering us another site to suit our 22ft van.
Camp Crusty supplies fire pit and wood which is so nice to sit around on cool nights.
We look forward to coming back soon.
Caravan Park is very old and poorly maintained. Amenities block was the worst we have ever experienced in 46 years of travel in Australia.
Run down. Place looked deserted. No grass all dirt. Amenities terrible. Didn’t stay. Awful!!!

The strangest/most hostile experience I've ever had at a caravan park.

*Arrived at 4:17pm*

Me: Hi there. Would you have an unpowered site for tonight please?
Manager: Bookings are online only.
Me: Oh, no worries - I'll jump on now.
Manager: No, we closed at 4pm.
Me: Can I just give you cash then?
Manager: No. We don't take cash.
Me: So... can I get a site?
Manager. No. We're closed.
Me: So you're here, I'm here, there are dozens of sites available... but I can't book in?
Manager: No. We're closed.
Me: (Gobsmacked)

*I move to the picnic table in front of the office and open Wikicamps to find alternate accommodation*

Manager: You can't stay here. We're closed.
Me: Yep, you've made that clear. I'm just madly looking for another camp site before everything else closes.
Manager: You're not a paying guest here so please leave.
Me: I would LOVE to be a paying guest here, but you won't take my money!
Manager: This is private property. Leave NOW!
Me: (Mind properly blown 🤯)

I've never felt so unwelcome anywhere in my life. I left feeling really quite upset... and almost threatened. It left me quite shaken.

Worst camping experience ever at Camp Crusty in Bright. The manager was completely unreachable — never answered the phone during or after hours, which made dealing with issues impossible. Other campers were cooking, drinking, and laughing loudly well past midnight, and there was no intervention or enforcement of quiet hours.

The toilet and shower facilities are incredibly outdated, seriously resembling something from the 19th century. Parts of the site feel like they’ve been untouched since the gold rush era — and not in a charming way. Definitely wouldn’t recommend unless major improvements are made.

We have had a wonderful experience for a 3 night stay at Camp Crusty in our self-contained van. Yes it is rustic however the amenities are very clean. Love the space, the trees, it's quiet, and that it's so close to town. We would definitely return. Our experience has been that management and staff have been very friendly and helpful. Great place to base yourself for exploring! Stephen and Leena Spencer, Tin Can Bay Qld.
I had an unpowered camp site, plenty of space and nice hot showers.

Great location for events like Buffalo Stampede and the bike tracks. We booked a hut but would definitely bring the van next time. Cabin was really disappointing for the price. It could be a really great spot with some TLC. If you book a cabin make sure to bring everything you need because you won’t get a cup or cutlery in there!

Great spot to stay whilst exploring the area.

Update in 2025. Weve been coming for a few years now when we have running or bike events on in the area. The recent upgrades are fantastic whilst retaining the classic charm the park is known for.

Staff are amazing & also check out the apples if in season on offer from the local orchard. Amazing job guys!

We love coming to Camp Crusty.
We’re always warmly welcomed back by Reenie & Damien.
Where else can you gather a big group of friends together to go Cycling, trail running, Mountain Biking, swimming, visiting the brewery or getting great coffee from the local roasters Sixpence and come back to bask in the sunshine at the base of Mystic Mountain.
Our only expectation is that we will have quality time with our friends and know that our kids are safe riding all over the campground without the fear of being run over. Bonus having Morses Creek right next to the campground to cool off in after a day of adventures.

Cheap accom, clean room, big deck, amaze location for MTB, good/basic facilities - but thin walls and noisy b/n adjoining cabins!
Room #40 has gaps around windows & door, letting Mosi’s in all night!! 🦟🦟🦟🦟🦟🦟
= 2x crap nights sleep!! 🤯😱🤯😱🤯😱🤯
Room #40 also next to septic 💩💩💩

Will stay again just in a very diff cabin!!

DO NOT STAY HERE!!!!!

As we entered the site, we immediately realised we had made a mistake. The camp site itself is void of any character, the amenities are disgusting (see pictures attached), and the other guests gave us an extremely poor impression - food waste and full bins left lying around attracting pests. The site is also unsafe - almost no lighting considering there’s big logs and rocks around the site and it’s very uneven ground.

The cabins are falling apart. It’s the most basic accommodation I’ve ever seen. We were even worried as to whether the sheets were clean.

The location isn’t what it’s made out to be either. It’s not within walking distance of the town centre, a car is definitely required.

It may be cheap, but it’s still too expensive for the quality. Paying a little bit more to stay somewhere else is most definitely worth it. It was by far the worst accommodation around!
Quiet park at night.

Do not stay here if you want a self contained room, any peace and quiet to sleep, want to roast all night in a Dutch oven or your room to flood. For $150 a night we at least expected a cup to be able to make a cup of tea, no plates no cutlery, no bowls. Camp kitchen doors, which one would assume you have access too to use crockery & cutlery like any other normal camp park, were closed - zero access. Only access is outside with bbqs and a fridge etc. Make sure you pack everything you require. The room is falling apart, windows are held open by a piece of wood which one wouldn’t even work, the tin roof of falling off and 4 rooms are crammed into one block. The room was disgustingly hot with only one fan for 2 adults and 2 kids, zero room to have bags let alone tripping over each others due to the tiny size of the room. No fly screens so bugs galore if you want to sleep with the ONE window open. The only way you can lock your door is via a pad lock - yes a pad lock literally is your safety measure with a gate lock & then a pad lock with a key. If you want to leave the room whilst others stay in there, the door has to be locked from the inside to stay closed but then the person on the outside can’t get it & vice versa (smart right) did I mention it flooded - yep zero drainage that works and a poor lady was out there with her shovel trying to save our rooms which I would imagine is an ongoing occurrence. Holes in walls bogged up by random things. The bed Mattress for the kids was so thin it may as well not have been there. The double was insanely uncomfortable with springs sticking into your body all night, very pain-full to be honest. The walls are paper thin so you can expect zero sleep and many party goers at your window until 3am despite asking them very politely twice to move over to the dismal camp kitchen so they can sit outside and my family could attempt to get some shut eye amongst the heat, bugs and noise. The whole park is on dirt, zero grass. Access to a toilet block is a fair hike and only 2 accessible for the WHOLE park not to mention that the whole park were crammed in like sardines on very small blocks. The ratio of humans to toilets was pathetic and lengthy line ups were very common. If you want a shower don’t bother it takes to long to be able to have one although once you had a shower that was the only posture thing about this stay, hot showers and powerful. We were absolutely blown away by the price for what we stayed in. Many properties are much better value for money. It was so bad that we left the night before check out and moved to a van park closer to town for $20 extra per night and it was pure perfection. We would have moved earlier but money was an issue given we had to eat 3 meals out per day as no way to make , prepare or cook meals. Don’t bother staying here!!!! Wish I had taken photos for proof.
Perfect, easy, good location, byo everything 😊

We come to Camp Crusty every March for the Brighter Days music festival and stay for a week. It is a beautiful spot and so close to the festival in Pioneer Park. Great to be able to walk to festival and back.
We love our stay here. It is very rustic. We caravan way down the front near the road and love our spot. We are self contained.
Will be great when (if) the council give the go ahead for the sewerage down the front and a dump point. Extra toilets would be good.
We look forward to our time here every year.
Good location to the festival very friendly owners who are always happy to accomodate only negative it’s very dusty

Fantastic time at crusty, only a half hour walk from town and right next to the chalets that take you up the mountain for riding. Was placed perfectly along the creek and had a site suited for our set up. Can’t wait to come back.

We have stayed a few times in this park with our caravan, we always love the walk into town in the mornings for a coffee run.
The Kids love the pump track across the road, easy location for bike rides.
Friendly staff here, always very accommodating.
We look forward to our next stay.
Great park! Friendly staff!

We had a terrifying experience at Camp Crusty when a tree fell onto our camper, cutting our trip short and leaving us to find emergency accommodation. While we understand that weather can be unpredictable, it was clear that many trees on the property were unstable and poorly maintained. Shockingly, management did not bring in an arborist to assess the remaining trees after the incident, raising serious safety concerns for future guests.  ||To make matters worse, the alternative accommodation we were offered was substandard—with holes in the doors, windows that wouldn’t close, and even bugs in the bed. It was nowhere near the standard of our self-contained van, and we were forced to pay for alternative accommodation out of pocket. Despite multiple follow-ups, management has refused to refund the nights we could not stay, which is extremely disappointing given the circumstances, and the fact we had stayed there for fiver years in a row. No goodwill at all!||This is not just a customer service issue but a serious safety hazard. If you’re considering staying here, be aware that tree maintenance does not seem to be a priority, and the risk to guests is real. I sincerely hope the owners take action before another tree falls—next time, it could be on a person.  ||Would not recommend until the safety risks are properly addressed.

Serious Safety Concerns – A tree fell on our camper and destroyed it. We are lucky we are alive!

We had a terrifying experience at Camp Crusty when a tree fell onto our camper, cutting our trip short and leaving us to find emergency accommodation. While we understand that weather can be unpredictable, it was clear that many trees on the property were unstable and poorly maintained. Shockingly, management did not bring in an arborist to assess the remaining trees after the incident, raising serious safety concerns for future guests.

To make matters worse, the alternative accommodation we were offered was substandard—with holes in the doors, windows that wouldn’t close, and even bugs in the bed. It was nowhere near the standard of our self-contained van, and we were forced to pay for alternative accommodation out of pocket. Despite multiple follow-ups, management has refused to refund the nights we could not stay, which is extremely disappointing given the circumstances.

This is not just a customer service issue but a serious safety hazard. If you’re considering staying here, be aware that tree maintenance does not seem to be a priority, and the risk to guests is real. I sincerely hope the owners take action before another tree falls—next time, it could be on a person.

Would not recommend until the safety risks are properly addressed.
Another great stay at beautiful Camp crusty! Can’t exaggerate how amazing this place is and the accommodation that comes with it. Highly recommend Camp Crusty if your looking for a place to stay.

My family and I stayed here recently. We absolutely loved the location , it was perfectly located to explore the beautiful area of Bright. The cabin we stayed in was basic but clean and reasonably priced and  the hosts were very welcoming and accommodating. We will definitely be back to explore the area more and will definitely stay there again

We had a lovely stay at Camp Crusty.
It was fairly basic which suited us perfectly.
The staff were very friendly and the amenities were clean.
Happy campers here.
Classic but clean old camp. Very simple but everything you need for a weekend bike run and walk around.

A pretty awful experience. The only redeeming quality of this campground is its proximity to the Mystic bike tracks - everything else is a complete disappointment. The grounds are run-down, barren, and utterly devoid of charm, making for a bleak and unwelcoming stay. To make matters worse, the camp manager was unreasonably difficult. It’s shocking to see a business run with such a lack of care, hospitality, or basic customer service. Given the poor conditions and the unfriendly atmosphere, this place is a complete waste of money. I'd recommend avoiding this campground.

I payed $110 for the heritage hut and I didn't stay. I turned around and went home. It's one thing for accommodation to be budget,  but this place isn't even that. It's not maintained at all. There's no lighting to navigate your way around the camp-site or the amenities, there's no grass, it's all dirt like you're in the wild west.  In winter I can only imagine the mud bath. Aesthetically it's not even relaxing. I'm 5'4 and the queen bed is too small for me. The photos on their website are filtered. I've stayed in caravan parks, in a swag, and it was better than this. Trust the reviews on TripAdvisor because it's definitely more accurate.

I loved staying at Camp Crusty. I stayed in a little loggers cabin which is very minimal. (No wifi or ensuite here!) but that was what I wanted, just a warm comfortable bed and a place to sit, have my morning coffee (stovetop espresso on a gas burner ) and ponder which cycling route I was goi g to take . Somewhere to cook my meals and keep my food cold as well.
If you’re in a budget, I highly recommend these cabins.
To put into context, it was a very quiet week when I was there. The last night I stayed there another person stayed in the cabin next door and I could hear everything so it made me wonder what I would be like in high season.
I would definitely stay at Camp Crusty again:)
It’s a very rustic vibe.
There are more luxurious cabins but I didn’t need any frills.
.

I want to fair with this review, given the ultra low price.
We booked a triple room for our biking trip. We’d read the reviews and weren’t expecting much, thought it’ll at least be a laugh if it’s not very good. Well we got a big laugh, my sides hurt from how hard I was laughing and I was out of breath.
The cabin was quite a small room, 1 half of a little logger hut positioned just behind 4 dumpsters. Ah the sweet smell of rotted garbage and dead rat. The door was held closed by using a gate latch on either the front or the back, no door handle. It was fine, but the one on the outside made me nervous that we could be easily locked in which is not safe at all. The door also had large gaps around the edge for bugs to easily get in, but being cold that wasn’t an issue for us.
The beds were of a reasonable size although pretty old and not very comfy. There were windows with blind, but they didn’t open for airflow. Later we moved to a different room, no blinds on those windows but the door shut much better. There was a pedestal fan, bar fridge and toaster. The cabin itself is at least 50 years old, probably more so no insulation, we slept very hot at first without ventilation and then very cold without insulation.
The amenities were basic, mostly clean but still in need of maintenance. I thought the sign on the back of the toilet stall door (pictured) was funny. Why not write a shitty review whilst you bathe in the smell of feacal matter?

We came back from dinner to discover one bed was ridden with bed bugs (pictured for evidence). We notified the manager who offered to move us to the room next door. We declined at first claiming that this showed unsanitary conditions and the next room over would be no different. He flatly refused our request of a refund claiming ‘well you guys have already checked in’ and ‘other people have stayed here and not complained’. No reader, you’re not not confused, it does not make any sense.
After a lightly heated discussion, It was clear we were never going to get any money back from this place so before we tried to find another place to stay at 9pm, we said we would check out the cabin next door. We carefully inspected the beds (couldn’t find any nasties in this room beside a few healthy looking spiders) and relented that it was better than sleeping in our cars.

So the fair part:
It’s cheap for a reason, don’t expect the world. Probably met our expectations for the price, other than the bed bug situation.
The amenities are usable and clean on our visit, but it’s nothing special. We didn’t use the kitchen so can’t comment on that. Location was great. Probably better for campers than cabins, but clearly they get used and I think it is good that there is this option for people on a lower budget. Not much else at this price point in Bright.

If you’re not willing to put up with all this, probably best you go elsewhere and pay a bit more. If you don’t mind any of that, well Camp Crusty might be for you.
Very nice place

One word for the cabins here: grim. Being given a padlock for our door shouldve been the first warning sign.
Room was barely sanitary with many bugs and spiders on the beds and living in the windows. The only air circulation was a pedestal fan to move around the stuffy hot air all night. They would be a health risk with how quickly it heats up in there. Door doesnt seal so the gaps allow for any mozzie to get in during the night and feast on you.
Overpriced fibro shack.
Better off camping or go somewhere else.

We booked a short stay and had to cancel it after having to pay in full. My 85 year old father has ended up in hospital with shingles and an undiagnosed heart problem. Anyway after several calls and emails all we got back was an email saying reference to our terms and conditions. We are disgusted in there lack of empathy. One can only imagine the type of owners/managers running this business.

Great spot especially if you are heading to mystic! Obviously a work in progress which is not a negative at all and prices reflect this as its substantially cheaper than other camp grounds over the holiday period and plenty of space for our large caravan setup. Thanks we will be back

We stayed in the tiny house over the weekend, as the kids were mountain biking at Mystic Moutain. It was great being so close to the Mystic shuttles, we overlooked the river. The tiny house was tiny! But it was perfect for the weekend. It's an easy 4 minute drive into the town of Bright. We had great customer services from 'Tupps' and all in all a brilliant stay. Will definitely come back.

Keep It Crusty!!!!|A lot of folk bag out this place as its not modern and it is basic. If you want all the mod cons go look elsewhere. If, however, you want an edge of town campsite at the base of the Mountain bike park and 5 mins walk to the paragliding landing field then this place is the go. Comparative to camping fees elsewhere in Bright this place is the cheapest and its super convenient for MTB/Paragliding stuff. Most fellow campers are here to do activities, it isn't crammed with families and kids like the in-town sites by the river. The staff are friendly and there is a lovely river right next to the camping.|Long Live Crusty!!!!

Cabin is great, clean and nice view.
However, tiny little details to improve :)
-No wifi - not even for TV
-A stove would be great for the full cabin experience, at least we can cook egg for quick breakfast
-There are 3 tiny cabins, no number on the door. we have to count down from the first one.

Very basic camping which worked for us as we were here for the MTB riding would probably give a higher rating if the camping fees were half the price
$20 a night for 2 people and I would happily come back $ 48 is just wrong 😑

We stayed in one of the eco cabins for 3 nights over New Year.  The cabin was excellent, the bed incredibly comfortable, it was very clean and had lovely touches.  The view from the little deck out to the woods was a lovely place to enjoy a morning coffee or evening drink. The showers were clean and powerful and the surrounding area and town of Bright are wonderful places to visit. We had a lovely stay.

We stayed here for 1 night in our tent+caravan and had a good view of the beautiful mountains, it has a beautiful creek that runs down the back. The shower had great pressure and toilets and showers were clean. Was nice and quiet. Although a bit older there was nothing wrong with the facilities we used and it was a quick drive into the beautiful town.

We came to Bright to ride our mountain bikes so this was the closest place to camp.
On the morning of the 6th of January 2025 we arrived to Crusty camp ground in the morning and went for a ride.
On that day it rained a lot and the staff were doing some cleaning, suddenly the general manager treated us very disrespectfully saying that we had to book on line before using the facilities.
I guess they can improve on their customer service , the cleanness , the quality of the facilities and the ambient in general.
I would rather sleep on my car at the parking lot of the mountain bike park next time.
Thank You for not much
Dusty, rusty, crusty guys

We have done a group booking for long weekend in Jan.
Can’t get onto anyone to check WHERE our powered site is, can’t get an email reply. No response from instagram page. Can’t call and leave a message.
And we HAVE PAID!!!!
Extremely unreliable

Bright, indeed. *Bright*ly overpriced, that is. Emphasis crusty...

Where to start? Oh, right—let's talk about the "air-conditioned" cabin. Because nothing says “luxury” like sweating in 35-degree heat with the promise of cool, refreshing air… that never arrives. Instead, we were handed a relic from the past—a loggers hut that looks like it’s been around since the dawn of time, with holes in the walls big enough to introduce us to a variety of disease riddled mozzies.

Forget the "private bathroom"—unless you're into sharing your space with a whole bunch of friendly men. No, seriously, we thought we were getting a chic little cabin. Instead, we got the joys of communal living with a dash of *lack of privacy*. The boys were lovely, but let’s just say, the “private” part of “private bathroom” was an afterthought.

Two beds, a sad little fan, and a glorious window situation that was more of an open invitation than anything else. Also, the curtains? Pathetic. You could practically see the years of neglect hanging off them. And don’t get me started on the "thunder box" they proudly call a public bathroom. For $325, I was expecting at least a *slightly* better experience than camping in a box with a fan. But hey, at least we had four walls! What more could one possibly need?

Avoid this place unless you're looking for an overpriced lesson in *how not to do hospitality*.

We had a great stay at Camp Crusty.  I agree with previous comments, this is a basic and mostly old camp ground, facilities are minimal and mostly very old and it's all a bit "Crusty".  However, this did not bother us mostly.  A few suggestions from us...
Get a dump point or advertise where one is located in Bright - we saw a camper dumping their cassette into the toilet directly and although they did not make a mess this is EXTREMELY dangerous for the entire camp, many people could get very sick.
Staff need to be able to talk through issues with customers nicely - one my my kids set up their tent in the wrong location and i happen to see a staff member place a negative note on the tent.  I approached them and tried to start a conversation/apology which they promptly turned on their heel and left without a word - certainly left a bad feeling.
Maps need more detail for where sites can access water and power - when we arrived the power for our site had been taken by our neighbor (they did not realise their power was in a different location) and we could not find out where we were to access water as all water taps had been connected to by others.
The park is a great location for mountain bikers.  We were sorry to hear more than half the park is set for subdivision for housing.
Awful. Definitely worst camp site ive stayed at that ive paid for
Crusty by name, crusty by nature. One hot, exposed, giant dust bowl.

Crusty is definitely one word for it.
We stayed in a “cabin” for one night and it was horrible - would not recommend.
Very overpriced for what you get.
The whole place is a dust bowl, so the floors were dusty and dirty, cobwebs all over the place, huge gap between the door and the frame (so bugs could easily get in) no top sheet just a doona in 35degree heat.
Fan was almost useless and the windows don’t open.
Bathroom facilities are old and a mishmash of materials to be one complete facility but clean enough.

Not sure what it would be like to camp there with all your own facilities - it seemed very popular for new years.
But with scraps, machinery and rubbish all over the place it wasn’t a very nice environment.

Close enough to town but too far to walk in summer heat.

Stayed at Camp Crusty several times over the years and I don't mind the rustic facilities and kind of casual nature with which the park operates. However be wary during high season/holidays as the place gets busy. There is no gate or any management of vehicles inside the park. We were litteraly surrounded by big 4WDs, Vans and cars blocking thouroughfares, camping outside sites (rooftop tents) and access for other users throughout our stay. It was like camping in a car lot! Not only was this just unpleasant and annoying but also presents a major safety issue within the park for any emergencies or potential accidents.  It should be one site one vehicle, not a free for all. Disaster waiting to happen.
Crusty is crusty and that’s why we love it.
The camp is simple and clean. Everyone was respectful. It was close to town without being in town.
Ok place to camp but the facilities are massively disappointing for what you pay, hardly any toilets or washing up stations so when it's busy be prepared to wait a long time..

Don't fall for their false advertising! They did build 6 new cosy private cabins they use to advertise but during the booking process somehow you get an old tiny one of 4 cabins with no privacy, no mosquito nets and a broken lock. That was a very unpleasant surprise at check-in and not for us only, for all the guests stayed next to us. One guest just opened this cabin - and left immediately to stay at his friends, another couple - never returned for their paid 2nd night - obviously they didn't get any refunds and it is extremely overpriced for what you get! It costed us like a proper motel room however, shouldn't cost more than a powered camping site.

Crusty is as Crusty does.  Don't expect all the bells and whistles here. It's not for you princess campers. However, if you are looking for a no nonsense, inexpensive place to stay, this is your go to - especially if you ride a bike.  We camped in cool shade under beautiful trees - so I can't say what the cabins were like. Amenities are primitive but really clean, showers hot with great water pressure. Ground was super hard, bring yr muscle and strong pegs. All in all   - a great addition to our camping adventure.

We had an awesome long weekend at Camp Crusty during the Bright Hot Rod show. We were close enough to be involved when we wanted to, and far enough away that we weren't caught up in the car parades unless we wanted to be.
Dust was a bit of a problem, at times, but all of the amenities were super clean and toilets were well stocked with toilet paper, soap and paper towels.
The Camp is in a beautiful setting. Being able to watch the parachutes launch from the top of the hill behind the camp was a lovely bonus.
