Had a ball, cheap acomadation great location, see you next year.

Tiny house was good but just don't bring too much stuff as you won't have room. Some small shelves in bathroom would be a plus and a power point for toaster and jug as microwave has the only one on opposite side of sink
Absolutely loved our stay here. The bed was really comfortable. Facilities were amazing and clean. Will definitely be back!
A space for camping with middle comfort, the few bathroom needs to improve.

I didn't like my stay at Camp Crusty , sorry. The communication with the management was very poor and when I arrived I had no idea where I was staying. The cabin was very basic, with no room to move around, very old, uncomfortable beds. I don't recommend it.

As a repeat customer for 2 years now, Camp Crusty always look after us. Whether we are in Bright for Mountain Biking, here for a long time or even just dropping by, the team always take our needs into consideration.

The pricing is also very good. Even if you book last minute. We normally just camp on unpowered sites therefore I cannot speak for the cabins.

Especially since we have a rooftop tent, they never fail to put us on a nice and flat surface.

In the case our site doesn’t suit, the team is always eager to reach our satisfaction and move us around if they’re able to.

The facilities are great too, always clean and monitored by staff. Their noise curfew is pretty on point to the fact the camp is pretty quiet and team reinforce to all campers that it’s 10pm, quiet time.

The place has definitely had some hard work put into it since the first time I ever stayed here and it’s really coming up nice. We can’t wait to see how it pulls up when all the work is done.

All in all, we would highly recommend this camp to friends, we already do, especially for mountain bike riders.

Dirt everywhere

Not the best place for families. Especially if you dont do mountain biking.
There are a few newer cabins which look very nice, looks like there is only 5 and they are for 1 or 2 people only.
Unfortunately we were placed probably in the worst spot. The building is very very old and cold. The beds are just a plank of wood with a thin mattress on top.
But the main thing is the dirt. If you want green space, playgrounds for the kids and rooms with no dirt inside this is not for you.
Just walking around lifts a lot of dirt from the ground and it makes its way into the cabins very easily. Now imagine with the cars and mountain bikes driving around!
The website advertises that they have a pozza oven available for guests, well when we asked where it was we were told the pizza oven has been broken for a while and they havent fixed it. And they havent taken it down from their website either. We brought ingredients for a pizza night and we had to improvise in the end.
Lastly we went out one day and when we came back to our room my son was able to open the door with the lock still on. The locks are so old that they are a bit bent so there is a way to open without having to use the key… pretty poor security, considering that the main entrance is free for everyone (no gate or any security at the front)
Their website looks very nice. But the photos are very different from reality.
Dissapointed and defintely not coming back

Owners should change business strategy and rent the place out as a set for horror movies.

Our cabin wasn’t ready when we arrived, and possibly hadn’t been reset for occupancy. On prior visits there was dishwashing kit as part of the cabin gear - this wasn’t available this time. The cabin has an ant issue that needs some attention. The TV remote is either dead or for another TV.

Great camp with an awesome vibrancy, let down on this visit by what seems like a failed cabin reset.

We Booked 3 nights in a powered caravan site… $170, and we needed to cancel as our family were in mount beauty. we were only a few hours outside of the 24hr cancellation window but told it couldn’t be done!!!!
Given that you’re so busy all year(as I was told) surely you could have easily rebooked that spot??  Need a bit more flexibility camp crusty!!

Site 73 - terrible corner spot - no shade and our van was Ike an oven every afternoon!
When we were setting up there were not enough water tap points for water, and were told we needed to supply our own connector to share with the people on the site next to us,, after lengthy discussions they provided one so every one had access to water! Thankfully!

Sites are too close to one another and any cars or bikes going past corner site 73 got the full brunt of dust and grime constantly! Maybe consider gravel on the roads to reduce dust in hotter conditions or bitumen?

Neighbours having a fire bucket fills your van full of smoke and the no noise after 10.30 pm rule doesn’t work at all!!
People partying carrying on til all hours of the early morning!

All up very disappointed with what we got for our money, hospitality not great, facilities just ok.

Only positives  - close to town for walking or bike riding everywhere.
Heaps of great bike tracks to venture off for the day.

Not a great spot to just sit and relax or enjoy your surroundings, plenty of other options might be better suited for all.

One of Australia's  worst campsites.
Zero grass,
Horrid amenities  that haven't  been serviced  since their inception in what looks like the 1970s.
Over priced land for camping on.

DON’T DO IT!!!! Steer well clear of this place. Unpowered sites are a joke, basically a small section of grass on the nature strip (!) at front of park (not even in the fenced area of park which I can’t imagine being legal), with the noisy road directly in front. Paid $120 something for 2 nights, and drove right off. Owners refused a refund when I complained. Should not be legal to leave this place open, it’s a disgrace, dirty and a downright rip off. Plenty of free camping all over bright and surrounds. Why bother!!! Don’t want to see the owners pathetic responses here either.
The new Eco Pods 👌👌

Stayed for 10 days. A great base for road riding, mtb, hiking, close to town. Like bush camping but with power and plenty of hot water. The camp kitchen is simple but has all of the basics. For the price, this worked fine.
Rustic campground with plenty of space and great location  and views - my new happy place
What a dump. Do not stay here.
It wasn’t very appealing, we realise there is work in progress and the serenity is fabulous.  and ammenities are all good. We loved it thanks Not cramped in. Like other parks.
What the room photos was shown is not what I got. The rooms were tacky and no locks.

Easy to find the place.room is clean and simple.
Get what u paid for.
Probably under renovation, not many cabin occupied.
Obviously, the site is in an outdoor location, so you wouldn't get the most comfortable stay. It's good for those who wanted a budget friendly accommodation and wanting to save money.
Shocking set up. Mud everywhere and nothing for kids. No lighting to get to bathrooms and heavily overpriced for what you get. Very disappointing.

Great location for Mystic , but showers aren’t something my misses likes , if you want a late check out so you can pack up after riding it needs to be organised prior as they always so no even if no one is coming

Camp Crusty is Beautiful Bright at its best! Perfect location at the bottom of Mystic. Picturesque camp grounds with Ren and Damian being the most friendliest and helpful hosts you could ask for. Thank you for another awesome weekend. We’ll be back!

Camp crusty is a great spot to camp while exploring the mountains of bright. The facilties are  perfectly clean and there are many cubicles. Camp crusty ofers a wode range of facilities from powered camping sites to beautiful cabins right on the river. I 100% recommend spening your camping days at this wonderful carvan park.

Very basic but that's what you want when camping in a park.
Staff are friendly.

Great camp ground.
Lots of improvements underway but clean, tidy and close to the trailheads.
I will stay/camp here again
Camp crusty is fantastic! Lots of renovations happening and a sensational spot for keen mountain bikers! Close to shuttles and plenty of space to enjoy. Thanks to the friendly and helpful staff too
A beautiful modern clean tiny home, well suited to our weekend away.

We have now been twice once 2 years ago and this time 5 nights in Aug 24
Booked a room for two and a caravan pitch
We come for the snow for a week or so.
The place is a beautiful location to the town and close to the river for walks with our dogs
We have had little to no contact with the staff
Toilets are clean and hot water was good
The cabins are old worn but warm and dry
The caravan pitch was a little muddy but fortunately not puddling this time around
Points we feel they could do better
The drive way needs attention a simple job of popping some gravel on it fill the puddles in we appreciate there is on going work on improving to the place but just a simple few truck loads of gravel for paths and driveways would make a dramatic difference
Lighting would also make a significant difference to campers comfort as there is none presentl
Don't go here.  It's a mudpit with unsafe rooms and danger tape all over the grounds.  What a disgusting place.

Love the ambiance. Has real Australian bush feel from the 1920’s or gold rush era. Authentic, peaceful but so close to town you are only minutes away from quality restaurants or shopping. Staff are freindly and helpful

If I could give this place 0 stars, I would. We were charged $113 per room and due to their so called “construction”, we were given the worst rooms imaginable. The rooms were so small that there was literally no space to turn around. If they are aware they have construction work why even leave the rooms open for bookings knowing there could be possible restrictions to doing their work with the weather during winter? We never got the original rooms we booked.
The worst part of all we were told a refund of $10 only could be processed for the tiny rooms they could provide. Also this was all last minute notice as we received the call on the day of the trip.

The pictures they post online are completely misleading.The bathroom and toilet situation was appalling with 3 out of 4 commodes clogged completely. In the map they provided us it states there are two toilets but there was only one toilet for all the guests who stay overnight. It looks like this place is not maintained one bit. The level of hygiene is unacceptable for the amount we paid. We went to the snow and had no issues there but we had to come to this camp site to get our vehicles dirty. It would be nice if they can first spend some time fixing the walking paths with some grass so that we don’t have to walk in mud. We went to a different camp site recently and the whole camp site was fully grass which was nice compared to this site. It seems like all they care is about earning money. If you run a business and charge a high amount at least the least you can do is deliver. If you come across site please do not book this. I have friends who are going to the snow and I’ve ask all of them not to choose this camp site! I’ve posted some pictures below and that’s how small the room is for two people for $113.

Not only crusty, but also rustic! Some old factory flair.

All the basic needs are there, and cooking/eating outside in the frost is really cool for those who like it.

Great customer service! Was panicking because I needed a place to keep my car for a few days while I visited my friend in the mountains, thankfully! Team crusty answered my call and swiftly booked me in, even putting my camp site behind the house just in case!
Very bad place to stay, not keep up to there on commitments, cabin are so old and broken, place is in muddy puddles

The team at camp crusty were super nice! Upon arrival our powered camp site was easy to find and park in. The space was undergoing renovations but was still nice and tidy. The facilities provided hot water for showers which worked like an absolute charm! The town centre was a short drive away close to food and shops.
There was also firewood available for purchase which saved a trip into town.

Will definitely be back on my next trip around. :)

This place is not close to the beautiful pictures put up .The room was dusty and dirty .No towels are provided but not mentioned on the website .The bathroom was a long walk through construction site and not safe for children.  I will not stay there again. The bed linen was dusty and dirty. A man in passing said, "Lucky you brought an extra blanket." I used these so my son could get some sleep.I have traveled a lot, and this is by fare the worse .👎🏼
Location is perfect for someone who want to explore the Australian Alps, but rooms and facilities as well customer service requires improvement

Accommodation details as posted and correct. 10am quite rules work for all, and everyone obeys that rule. Great sense of understanding. Bathroom facility can be improved, I am sure the other improvements happening now, will just make everything better.

Basic accommodation that's comfortable  , right next door to MTB park. Sam was a great host who looked after our group.
Looking to return soon

This place doesn't pretent to be something that it's not. It's a charming destination for the likes of my 14yo son and his friends who had a magical experience at Mystic Mountain. Thanks to Cath at CC she was helpful, courteous and geneorus with her time .

Booked a spot here due to its proximity to Mystic Mountain Bike Park. Upon arrival we found it quite difficult to find our designated spot due to poor sign writing and overgrown foliage. We wanted to have a fire but couldn't believe it was over $20 just to hire a pit and another $20+ for firewood. We end up just going to the pub.  The facilities were also mostly out of order.
I just found the experience overall very average, next time we will stay at a free camp down the road and drive to Mystic. This park is in an amazing spot right across from Mystic trails but is really let down by it's upkeep. Waste of potential.

Called camp CRUSTY for a reason. 30min walk from town, facilities are horrible, only one of the men’s showers worked and they stared chainsaws and mulches at 7:30 in the morning!! Would highly recommend a place closer to town! Just pay the little extra for more comfort.

Perfect location for easy access to Mystic mountain bike park. The tiny home we stayed in was very clean and nice however we had issues not having any water available in the mornings due to the frost and the pipes being frozen. This park will be a beautiful stay when all renovations are complete

Absolutely appalling “cabin” -no curtains (mold on the wi does) with a spotlight lighting up our room all night, a two inch gap all around the door, bolts to keep the door closed (other occupants had to be bolted in when someone left the cabin-great health and safety there!) , neighbours that made a racket at night for hours.
Bathroom sinks that came away from the walls. These “cabins” need to be bulldozed. Molly, falling apart.
Was threatened with two pieces of wood by a drunk, violent guest and so called management wouldn’t do a thing about it! No refund given even after we had to flee in the middle of the night as we feared bf or our safety. Avoid this place like the plague!
If I could give negative stars I would! Most awful place !

The location was perfect, 10min ride into town, situated in a quiet area. Reccomend Camp Crusty for travellers looking to spend some time on their mountain bike or sit back and relax. Staff were around at all times and happy to help. The bathrooms were old school had great showers and kept tidy. Powered camp ground was reasonably priced. Cheers for the stay

We enjoyed our stay here. Short drive to town. I like the contactless arrangement. Our cabin was clean and so were the amenities. Does need a lick of paint, but it accommodated our needs for a clean bed (which had an electric blanket), a roof and a clean working toilet all for a good price. Short drive to town.
Fantastic tiny house experience.

I booked a "cabin", which could more accurately be described as worn-down shed (complete with a latch on the outside should some annoying person wish to trap you in the shed). The windows were cracked and didn't close properly, the door had seemingly been bashed in and repaired with a flimsy bit of plywood, and place generally gave off a vibe of people trying to pass on their trash as treasure for cash. The so-called "camp kitchen" is a sad cluster of weathered benches and dirty equipment outdoors on a concrete slab. At least it had a roof, I guess, but given that the ground is just hardened dirt, this offered little protection from grime. Don't get me started on the toilet block. I can't even say it was good value for money as I've stayed in places in the region that were far better for a few bucks more per night.

Very friendly, facilities very old but really clean. Just stayed in my Motorhome and wasn’t too disturbed by construction going on around the park. Will look great when it’s all finished, good luck with the renovations you have a big job ahead of you, will come back and check it out in the future.
Love this place x the rooms are quite small and old, however so tidy upon arrival and came with electric blankets 😘 the views around this park are gorg and everyone is pretty nice
We've been coming to Crusty for years and it's always been spot on! Nothing better than a hot shower after a day on the bike!!

We stayed 2 nights with our caravan. It's a quirky place, located not too far from the centre of Bright. We cannot comment on the cabins. The amenities are basic but ok. It's a real shame though there is no grass, so we were camped right in the middle of dirt, which made for a very dusty stay. We'd hate to think what it would be like if it rains!  At $56 per night for a powered site, we expected something a bit nicer. The booking system was completely automated with online booking and email confirmation, so we had no interaction with staff. It was quite tricky to work out where the boundaries of our site were, especially as the site map that was emailed to us did not quite match up to the actual site locations. As it was, we were the only caravan there for the 2 nights, so the site boundaries were really not that important. A definite positive is that the place is pet friendly, so we were able to bring our little dog.
Had a powered site for my caravan, good location for the mountain bike riding.  I did love the old charm of the park. Reminds me of the old school and scout camps.

We felt scared and unsafe. Campers next to us were so drunk and loud. 1:00 at night they were so loud. Securities and police tried to stop them but can’t do anything. We stayed for 3 nights and raised the issue but never resolved. Didn’t even offer us to move to a spot far from them even if there are so many vacant spots.

One of the very few budget options available in Bright and the place reflects the low price. The cam groud is rustic, uneven with limited grass areas. We stayed in a double cabin which looked tired from outside, but inside the story is different. For the size of the cabin it has all the amenities a hotel would provide. There is a tv, fridge, fan, heater and a bed lamp. The bed and in fact the whole cabin was clean. The shared facilities were quite clean.

The good things stop here. Here are some things many may consider a deal breaker. The cabins have zero weather or sound proofing. The cold night we stayed there we shivered through the night. The heater provided was of not much use. Pets are allowed in cabins, there were two dogs in our next cabin which were noisy for some time in the middle of the night. There was construction noise throughout the day too. The reception staff were not the warmest and prefer no human contact as they prefer contactless check-in and checkout. However, they helped us with finding our cabin number which wasn't emailed to us. As per some reviews the keys can open any cabin lock, so safety is a concern if you keep anything valuable.

There are some new tiny houses which seems to be better options if available. Overall for the price we paid it is a great value accommodation in Bright. However, we would happily pay a bit more and get a better place next time.

It's a very good spot for MTB riders as the shuttle runs right next to the camp. The facilities were not too bad, but the power went off at night and didn't come back on for a long time. No one to ask for help on-site. You will freeze without power in Winter.

I stayed at Camp Crusty - Bright Caravan Park for two nights at a powered camping site. It was an event organized by my mates, and since it was my first time in Bright, Vic, I was excited to visit the place. Unfortunately, due to an unforeseen event, one mate who organised the event backed out, and we ended up visiting Bright with another mate who booked powered campsite 31 while I was booked in 32. Due to logistics constraints resulting from the cancellation, we ended up sharing my tent with the mate who booked site 31. Despite the logistical issues with tents, we proceeded with the plan and set up one tent in site 32, utilising site 31 for the fire pit.

We still had a relaxing time after enjoying a good pub meal in town on our first night, which was a Friday. We returned to our campsite around 9ish, had some drinks, and enjoyed relaxing music while chatting. As the night grew cooler, we wrapped up and probably slept around 11 PM. After a good pub meal and a few drinks, I quickly dozed off, enjoying the cold weather inside my camping sleeping bag.

However, a few minutes after I hit the sack, I noticed a bright light coming from someone's headlight beaming directly into my tent. I heard one of the staffs working on something in an old house closer to our campsite (site 32) location. Despite the late hour, around 11 PM to 12 MN, I found it very rude and disrespectful that the staff didn't exercise common courtesy to inform us, given that their lights were shining brightly toward our tent. I chose not to go out and engage in drama, wrapped up in my camping sleeping bag. As a paying customer at a camping site, I expect better treatment. I've camped in various places across Victoria but never experienced anything like this. Fixing things at that time was uncalled for, especially shining headlights directly into someone's tent when they are already resting. Exercising common courtesy before doing such tasks late at night could have made the situation more understandable. Overall, the experience didn't sit well with me, and I found the late-night maintenance disrespectful and not reflective of good customer service.

On my second night, I had a good time and a good sleep with no issues. Overall, I still managed to enjoy my experience in Bright and look forward to returning to explore more with my family and friends.

Great place to stay if you are in Bright for MTB due to the location at the base of Mystic and the relatively cheap prices for Bright.

The amenities are pretty decrepit, however they are clean, so while the prices are cheaper than other Bright accomodation it's still expensive for the state of the facilities. If you don't mind a dingy bathroom then it's fine.

Crusty is known to get pretty rowdy at night, but our experience staying here several times has been pretty chilled vibes.
Staff are lovely & very helpfull.  Thanks!!! will stay again.

We recently stayed at Camp Crusty with our two children and couldn't recommend this venue more! It's just out of town which was perfect for our morning walks and kept us away from the hustle and bustle. The camp facilities were clean and in working order. The team were amazing and so kind

If you are after very basic cheap accommodation it’s perfect. Amenities were clean, but do your research eg not all linen provided, camp ‘kitchen’ very very basic. It’s basically a bed in a very old hut. Communication good.

Unfortunately you can’t pick zero stars! This place couldn’t be more appropriately named! Forced to stay here as it was the only place available for the dates we needed to be in bright for work. Power outages 3 times in our 2 night stay. For $200 a night it was an absolute joke. Paid for a fire pit and wood only to have it stolen and management did nothing about it. Luckily we had it the one night because without power for 3 hours we would have froze. I felt sorry for the families with young kids. Overcrowded and completely run down. Sign board out front advertised wifi which they do not have. Rude staff. patched up ceilings(thank god it didn’t rain). We’ve shared our experience with all the locals and family & friends. Do Not waste your money! We couldn’t get out of there fast enough!
A great place with great stuff.

We were vey disappointed with our choice of caravan park. The sites are incredibly clustered and not matched to the type of equipment you bring.
Our small site was on a hill and not suitable for the camper trailer, we called up before booking to request a larger flat space and book with a person, and we were rudely told to just book online.
The showers have NO hot water, the facilities are revolting, dirty and too small. I don't believe they have been renovated or improved since they were built.
The sites are over priced and you have zero privacy, I would have preferred to free camp elsewhere. They really need to just pull the facilities down and start again.
I would choose a different park when visiting Bright. The location is the only thing keeping this place alive.
Worst camp site ever! Terrible facilities and horrible service!! Will never stay again.

We booked four nights in advance but after seeing the standard of the facilities decided it wasn’t for us. Receptionist said ‘once we arrive we are not entitled to a refund for any of the four nights’. Unfortunately the photos did not show the substandard shower/toilet block facilities. Appreciate this is a ‘bush camp’ but at $56 per night for a powered campsite the riverside NRMA campground down the road for the same price would have been a much better option. There was no wifi even though the sign at the front advertised wifi. The grounds and dirt roads are not well maintained. If your schtick is staying in a place reminiscent of a dirty dusty run down spaghetti Western movie set - you’re gonna love Camp Crusty!  On the Bright side the place was peaceful at night because there were not many others staying (unlike all the other busy caravan parks).
Rooms very basic but very clean toilet and showers and kitchen amenities

Overpriced for what you get. We were on a powered site so cannot make comment of what the rooms were like on the inside though on the outside they look like they need some maintenance re: roof and cladding. Although the amenities block functions it could do with an update e.g. holes in wall, exposed pipes, resealing of floors, benches to place clothes whilst in the shower. Also the front board noted there was WIFI accessible at the park, though we were unable to access it.

Okay time for some positives!!
It is dog friendly.
There is heaps of space between your neighbours so you are not on top of each other.
It is really peaceful as most of the guests are out bike riding.
You have a spectacular view of the paraglider's taking off and landing in a paddock walking distance from the caravan park.

The fibro cement tiny cabin and amenities block reminded me of school camp from 30 years ago, it was fun despite the large gap above the door and windows that didn't latch closed.
We genuinely enjoyed it.
For the money/cost it however it was unacceptable (over $100per night).
The name suits it's basic but at a price
Great Location set against the backdrop of the Mountain a short distance from town.
Camp crusty hits the spot for our family, as we want lower cost accom, maybe have breakfast there, and then eat out for lunch & dinner.  So, perfect for us.  Close to mountain bike shuttle - great!

This place was shockingly different to its pictures , it was like a prison camp turned into accommodation. We booked 3 night , swapped rooms twice due to terrible beds, mice, dog urine and noise and still left early after our second night. It is at least 70 years older than the pictures online. If we hadn’t driven from so far away we would’ve left after the first night. You really get what you pay for 🥲

Stayed 2 nights after easter. Ok campground but owner/manager are not very friendly or flexible, facilities are very dated and it is very expensive for what it is. We arrived at 4h30 pm with no booking on a Tuesday. Campground was half empty. Owner told us we were very lucky they were there to book us in and they made an exception to book us 1 night only (not their Easter min. of 4) on the Tuesday after easter. Charge 20$ for fire pit you need to carry yourself to your site and 40$ for wet wood. It is also located next to a logging haulage road, which can be noisy and dusty early in am. Plenty of nicer other options in town.

The photo of the Cabin looked impressive when making booking. Sadly it does not show the whole room. We expected at the very minimum a toilet, shower & comfortable seating.
The shared facilities like toilet & shower block is also below expectation.

Our family had a great time.  conveniently located near 4 wheel drive and bike tracks, and the ability to bring your dogs is great!  We didn't know this for our visit and we hate leaving our pooches at home, so next time, they'll be joining the family holiday!

Firstly, I couldn't speak more highly of the staff here, they do an excellent job and this is a big part of why we come back here every year (7 years and counting). The camp is on a beautiful spacious block surrounded by beautiful views, big gums and natives with a nice creek running by the grounds.
Prices are very reasonable with everything you need for a comfortable camping/cabin experience.
Feels a more free and laid back compared to the busy parks we stayed at in town in the past.
Think we might have to make this a twice a year trip.

After initially reading the reviews, was hesitate to book.
However, after spending 5 nights over Easter, I wouldn't hesitate to go back.
Very affordable and friendly/accommodating staff.
This is not a caravan park, it's an outdated, over cramped, camping ground. The space between sites is well below average. The amenities are terrible, and the water pressure is not much better.

We loved our stay at Camp Crusty, we enjoyed time at and in the creek.

The amenities may be old but they were always clean.

The grounds are hard and rocky so come prepared, that is soon forgotten while relaxing in the tranquillity of the surrounding nature.

Lots to do for the whole family, with mountain bike tracks and a short walk into town.

At the end the of the day you can sit by the camp fire (fire drums for hire) and look up to the beautiful clear sky filled with shining stars.

Connect with nature at Camp Crusty.

I loved this place in terms of locations and amenities, although there are some inconsiderate dog owners who have forgotten to clean up after their pets. I stepped on one of those and had to spend a fair bit of time to get rid of it from my sneakers and the car mat.
One of the best campsites in Vic! Thanks for another great weekend crusty crew

Great location. Short drive, bike ride or walk into town of Bright. Opposite Mystic Mountain. Ideal camp ground for dogs - beautiful parklands opposite for dog walks.
Facilities are clean- a little run down, but clean. Great shower pressure.  Lots of families. Quiet at night. No loud late night parties. A bit dusty as cars drive past - but otherwise a well maintained camping park.
Definitely recommend if you’re a happy camper and not too precious!!!
Arrived turns out management sent the wrong emails, asked about it and she denied it, horrible staff first impressions and small rooms

So rude and nasty ! We received instruction emails telling us the cabin numbers, and then 1 text message telling us contactless reception and another one telling us to see the reception. When we came, we noticed the cabin numbers are wrong. So far it’s ok. Everyone makes mistakes, it’s fine. But then when we tried to confirm with the lady “ were emails wrong?” All we got was “ your cabin s are there, go to your cabins, otherwise do you want my husband here to kick you out?!”
Where do those come from? !

Update:
And it turned out the key and room number are wrong again. And the lady said it’s the kids messed up with keys. Then some dirty words came out from the husband mouth.

Also, I was told one key can unlock all pods.

Not recommend.

Video here:

https://youtu.be/__WWNQgfMjY?si=MVwKU_yd1DQtqwb4
Great spot right near the trails, friendly staff and an old school feel that reminds me of camping when I was a kid, nothing flash but thats what makes it special.

Can't say it was the best but the staff were amazing.
If you need a cheap place to stay whilst exploring Bright then this is definitely an option.

We stayed in one of the older cabins which was rustic but clean and the beds were comfortable. The amenities blocks were also very clean. A great location, especially for mountain bikers.
Good value for money
Great location but not a monitored site despite being a paid one, our neighbouring campers were being really loud up and beyond midnight, so not a great place if you like yr sleep

Once again my visit to Camp Crusty was a 10/10 experience. Managers are amazing. Nothing an issue. Always friendly & so accommodating. Camp site is big enough for my car & cabana. Even though the ammenities may look a tad underdone the water is hot in the showers & the camp kitchen has everything you need for a night or five. Always wish I could stay longer. Until next year !!

It was a more pleasant experience than staying in town, quiet, peaceful and "rustic". Rooms were clean, showers had plenty of hot water and amenities were clean too.

I know the rooms are cheap, but it may be time for new mattresses.

Amazing staff, welcoming and warm, great location and the creek is absolutely amazing! Toilets and kitchen were so clean!
Will definitely be coming back!
Absolutely amazing experience! The creek was beautiful and mountain biking was so much fun! Staff were amazing 🤩 highly recommend

Camp site was just what we needed for a weekend MTB trip. Nice clean facilities - love a clean shower and toilet when camping! The staff were very knowledgeable and great to have a yarn with! We will be back!
Stayed for Brighter Days. Great place with excellent staff

Stayed in unpowered section. Ground was a little uneven and rocky BUT don't let that put you off (bring a hammer for your pegs and some extra padding for your swag). The camp ground is shady, open and in a great location for all activities that Bright, Beechworth and Milawa have to offer. Amenities are on the older side, but were always clean, plenty of loo paper and paper towel. It's clear to see that the owners are putting some decent money and time into improving the park. Manager was quick to reply to my email and very helpful with sorting out an issue I had with a double booking. The only reason I didn't give 5 stars was because reception/office is only open for an hour a day between 4pm and 5pm.

Camp crusty is an inviting Enviroment everything works so well. The staff are very friendly, welcoming and always ready to lend a hand.
Always an amazing place to stay to sit in In a cabin and lighter fire and have a few drinks with Friends would recommend to anyone.
Thanks for the staff Camp Crusty

Location of park is fantastic but overall I’m utterly disappointed. Camp sites are tiny. And we are literally camping on one another. We received plenty of notifications that keep the noise and music down after 10:30. But party’s happening way after 1/2am. The toilet block is a little run down but it’s not the worst either.
Just love everything about this place!
Just not finished yet far too packed

Excellent location with accommodating staff. Easily modified booking without any issues. Camp Crusty, offers basic yet clean facilities and a friendly atmosphere. Guests are generally respectful of noise curfews

We stay in this caravan park annually as we like the location. However, as a 25 yo woman, this year I’m thoroughly offended to have been asked by management to stay quiet so that she can “speak to my parents”. What was a constructive problem solving conversation with absolutely no conflict taking place turned into a very disrespectful and dismissive situation. I will certainly not be returning, as a self sufficient adult I would rather support other local businesses who respect me as a paying customer.
We spent an amazing Spartan weekend there. We only rented a powered space. All the facilities were clean and tidy.

We visited Bright while doing our Big lap. This was our last resort as other places could not accommodate. We were given a nice spot which had some grass as most camping spots are just dirt. The amenities need a massive upgrade. Compared to other parks we have stayed at this was the most expensive so far.

Cabin 4  1/3/24
Last minute booking , the only accommodation we could we could get that wad pet friendly , smoke and mirrors on line with the photos wide angle lens, no bigger than a walk in wardrobe ,hot day no fly screens , talk about run down , l could swear l heard banjo's in the distance, high tailed it out of their in two minutes, the dog was way ahead of us .
Nice little scam to pick up a quick $100.

Great location
Great amenities

Pulled up a swag on an unpowered site....beautiful location, felt one with nature 🙂.
Had everything if needed....kitchen facility, toilets, showers....even fire pits.
Great location for the Spartan race we attended.
Great friendly staff, went above and beyond to accommodate.

Had a great stay. Good location and staff are friendly.

Hail to thee, Camp Crusty,
By the shores of Big Snake Lake.
Though your swings are rusty,
We know they'll never break.
Close to trails

If you’re heading to Mystic Park to Ride MTB’s, I highly recommend staying here. Park management is friendly, helpful and does a great job making sure all guests have a fantastic stay. The location is great, and a more natural setting than staying in other parks in town.
Excellent location for paragliding and MTB trails, very noisy from road, old and worn out facilities but in process of updating. Owners friendly

The location is the only good thing about the place . The amenities are very old but clean . We had booked the double double hut . It was not a pleasant stay to say the least . The management would benefit if they tell the customers that they are really old cabins . My issue was not even the old cabin but the fact that the bed sheets were not clean . The rooms had a very strong smell . I told the staff and she said that they regularly change the bed sheets and if they didn’t it would have made her job so much easier. When requested for a fresh set , she was kind enough to bring it over .  It is an old place but if they tell the patrons before hands and clean the rooms a bit better , it is not too bad .
Cheap and cheerful. Great location. It has a good heart
Great laidback feeling

Great location if you enjoy mountain biking. Only a small ride from town too. The bike wash station was a great addition.

The camping spots are extremely crowded and uneven. Amenities are extremely old and run down, but in fairness kept really clean.

Staff were friendly but not present which led to multiple campers being noisy all throughout the night and keeping others up.

The most over priced camp we’ve stayed at. Ammenities are very run down, most doors in ladies toilets don’t lock. Men’s showers only have a curtain that blows in the wind and leaves you exposed.
Sites are small and close together.
For $50 per night for unpowered, we expected a bit more. Location was the best part about it.. very noisey at night but to be fair we stayed on a Saturday night.
It was really one of the best places to have some time off with family.

I had a highly disappointing experience at this camping site . Despite paying over $200 for a parking site for two nights, the stay was marred by a group of noisy teenagers who kept everyone awake until midnight. The lack of management response and poor sound insulation made it unbearable, especially for a family with a baby under 1 year old. The proximity to a main road only added to the discomfort. Not worth the money, and the lack of communication from the management team was extremely frustrating.

Sam was welcoming on arrival.
Camp Crusty is aptly named in a good way.
Very basic but clean with easy check in and out. We loved it

Great park, with exceptional managers.
Powered Van site for for 26 days of bliss.
Good value for mountain bikers

Great location with friendly staff that respond to queries in a timely manner.
We used a powered site for our caravan.
Amenities are in dire need of updating but clean and functional nonetheless. Well maintained.
Large camp sites are excellent for caravans, tents etc when you have loads of bikes with you.

Great base if you are riding Mystic.
Quiet when we were here, which was very unexpected.
Big storm passed through quickly, so be wary of loose branches in trees.

Being well situated a short drive from the town centre is the ONLY redeeming thing about this camp.
There is no grass. Only silty dirt that clings to everything. Not ideal for tent camping.  When it rains, it is a muddy mess.
There is NO HOT WATER plumbed to the 2nd of 2 amenities blocks. Would be nice if this was NOTED somewhere before people 'commit'. This means there are only 3 'proper' showers for the whole park.

I suspect it's Camp Crusty with a 'C' because Krusty himself would not want to be affiliated with this place.

We only booked a logger's hut as it was cheaper than camping for 2. Well worth it for when a big storm came through and a power line fell.

Facilties are very basic and old, but generally clean. Pallet beds with built shelving. At least 2 powerpoints. Fan, but small window without fly screen. Near bike trail and equipment to clean gear and laundry. Would recommend another laundry line or bringing your own.

Bare minimum of communication from team. Our neighbours were told power was out after the fact, but nothing proactive regarding access while power line repairs in process. Creek is close by and looks great to swim in. Would recommend if you literally just need to have a place to crash after big days out near Bright.

They do look like they have newer modern, accomodation being set up.  Roads a bit rough but manageable on site. May just be the result of recent flooding.

Unfortunately had a really poor experience here last year. We arrived after cycling the Murray to Mountains trail, it was 30 degrees and we were exhausted. We were shocked to see the camp had no shade. There are some tall trees but they are so bare they didn't offer an escape from the sun. As a result our tent was roasting hot, we couldn't stay in it. We had to cycle into bright to find shade and return at night. Amenities were basic but did the job. I don't think I will be returning here, our stay was not pleasant.
Stayed on a powered site. So close to Mystic park. The new facilities look good, hopefully the park will gradually update as some of the amenities are a bit aged.
A great rustic campsite ⛺️ a really relaxed place to chill and enjoy your break. Awsome location for bike riders
Our third stay we absolutely love Camp Crusty can't wait to return 👍😊

We stayed at CC for 12 nights on our caravan on a powered site. Easy booking online system and great communication re site etc. Easy map to follow to find yr siteExcellent location to town.. half hour easy walk or 10 min bike ride.. use the track through the park opposite for a flat ride to and from town. Excellent location for mountain bike enthusiasts as right at bottom of the Mystic Mountain trails and shuttle bus. Amenities are very old but clean. Seemed to be plenty of hot water although we used our own shower. Cabins are very old and looked pretty basic but they were pretty full most nights. People obviously happy to stay in them for a couple of nights. New ones have been built but not in use yet. We had an excellent site which was mostly grass.. plenty of room for us to have the awning out and park our wagon. We were able to put our grey water into the hedge behind us but most people have to just lay the pipe under the caravan which would be a negative. Lots of trees so plenty of shade. No dump station yet.. apparently they are putting one in this year but the manager told us that Tasman Park Camp would let us use theirs for $5 which they happily did. No dump station in town to use! Managers are onsite but we only saw them as they went about their business. Loved the relaxed feel of CC .. plenty of space .. didn’t feel cramped. Bernie’s creek is cute and nice for a paddle but plenty of excellent swimming holes around.. recommend the Tronoh Dredge hole at Harrietville!! Loved the easy access to the rail trails . Has a coffee caravan set up onsite but wasn’t open which was a real shame. Looks brand new. People were respectful of 10,30 quiet time which was great and good for young families. Has excellent laundry facilities.We throughly enjoyed our stay and would happily return sometime again!!

If you want a nice holiday. Do not stay here. From disgusting facilities to the disgraceful layout this place should open. No rules, no management. There is no dump point. This place should not be open. PLEASE DO NOT STAY HERE. Your dirty water goes all over the site. Endless partying. WHAT A DISGRACE.

Bitterly disappointed  ..  WE LEFT O  THE 2ND DAY OF OUR 3 DAY BOOKING.
The site we were allocated 107 blocked another site in and they could not get out  they left after our arrival and a day early on their booking.  Another concern was that extention cords were laid across the road to the power point. Both of these things were an extreme fire risk and an endangerment to campers. Not to mention when the rain came most were just laying in a pool of water.  Very few rubbish bins, badly defined roads, toilets and shower were clean but were in dire need of a rejuvenation. Would not go back to this establishment  would not recommend to anyone in fact l would tell them how bad it is.  I am surprised that they would pass any inspection for being able to run a caravan park for the public

We camp quite a lot over Victoria and other states, this is the first time I have ever left a less than favourable review of a caravan park. The cost is probably the main issue, it just isn’t reflective of the service and amenities offered, and I wonder if maybe this review might have been more favourable if the cost matched the site size and amenities available. Having recently travelled right up to Kings Canyon over peak Easter season, it was very surprising to have the cost of the campsites at Camp Crusty almost as much as luxury campgrounds near Uluru that included pools, playgrounds, jumping pillows, indoor kitchens and games rooms. Neverless, we booked ahead, there wasn’t anything else available that late in the year in the area for that time ( we booked Nov 23 for early Jan 24). When I arrived, I had just come straight from a big 4 in Bendigo that had a pool, spa, large indoor air conditioned play centre, outdoor playground, jumping pillow, indoor kitchen, indoor games room, and very large sites, and paid less per night. I get the location makes a difference to price, but so do that level of amenities, when Camp Crusty has none of them.

Over the phone a friendly female told us the campsites were quite big, and would fit large caravans on them. She said they were all the same size, so reassured us we didn’t have to worry what site we got, I had asked for big sites. This was not the case when we arrived. It was probably the smallest site we have ever been on, it was lucky we were camping next to friends and we could jigsaw everything to make it work, however, we could not fit our cars on there though. For perspective, we would have fit our small canvas soft floor camper trailer and a gazebo on our site only, not our car as well. Luckily for us, the park was reasonably empty, so we were able to park our cars on vacant sites across from us.

I’ve seen comments about the amenities, they’re pretty fine, not up there with big 4 level, but definitely not down there with the worst of them. Mid range reasonable amenities. But again, it’s the price that frustrates you, when you pay so much per night, you kind of expect a higher level of amenities. The male toilets only have material shower screens, not lockable doors, so may be a safety issue for parents with children, although you are required to supervise them.

One night we went to cook toast in the two toasters for our dinner, both of them weren’t working, we had bread instead. Like I said, usually this stuff wouldn’t bother me too much when I’m paying $$ matched for the value offered, but these guys charge more than big 4s, and don’t offer anywhere near the same level of service.

Another night there was a campsite near us that was pretty loud way past the noise curfew time, it doesn’t seem like anyone is is around to manage this at night. Maybe they came a bit later.

Overall, it’s a great location, and a nice reasonable park, really awesome creek down the side of the park too. We love the area, happy to pay those prices though for a few more amenities and larger campsites.

We don’t normally write reviews as your holiday adventures are what you make of them - not what happens or doesn’t happen at a caravan park.

However after seeing some of the horrendous comments, I figured I may as well share my experience to drown out some of the negative keyboard warriors.

The park is rustic and holds a lot of history in the local area. It is clearly written up on their website, so perhaps do your research and find a place that suits your personal needs & expectations before you book online.

This is our second stay in the park, the first in a camper over the Brighter Days Long weekend and now in a Caravan after New Year.

Yes the 1st amenities block is rustic but functional, however there is a 2nd amenities block less than 50 metres away which is clean, tidy and more updated than the original one.

If you stayed here in the last few months, you can see that there is new accommodation options and building works going on. Improvements take time.

The accommodation cost has been factored into the park being not as modern as others in the area. A simple google search on the other parks and what they are charging over peak periods without being dog friendly are obvious. $80 per night for unpowered compared to $49 per night is just one clear and factual example.

The park staff we dealt with were nothing short of friendly, accommodating and willing to help in anyway they could.

As for noise, all caravan parks get a little roudy over the peak periods. Yes loud music outside of the standard hours happens, so be a good camper and respect the rules and your camping neighbours. You wouldn’t do that to your neighbours at home, so why disrespect your fellow campers and ruin their time.

As the story goes, you get what you pay for - and as all the other parks are fully booked, charging well outside your budget or like us don’t allow you to bring your dog on holidays with you, just remember that staying at Camp Crusty has given you the ADVENTURES you were seeking on your holiday!

Thanks Camp Crusty for hosting us & our 4 legged friend

Have stayed at Camp Crusty half a dozen times. Facilities are very basic but generally kept clean and harbours a friendly atmosphere. Guest are typically respectful to noise curfews. Recommend reviewing the site map prior to booking to try and secure prime locations and adequate space for your party size.

Literally hell on earth.
Weridos muddy terrible infrastructure and just a big pile of slop on the side of the road In middle of nowhere with Hilltop Top Hoods blasting and they refused to refund even though we couldn’t even drive In because the puddles were so big not cool man why don’t you just refund us???

Very basic and clean cabin, cement outside the cabin could be a hazard as tree routes are growing underneath it not all the cabins have this but the cabins are clean and there is a table and chairs underneath an awning which is great.  Camp kitchen is great.
It is very run down. There were a lot of cockroach's in our cabin and a few in the bed. Good location and friendly staff. Expensive for the quality of the campground.

Great location. Staff were super easy to work with. We made a late change to our booking and there wasn’t a hassle. They did have security present over new years period to help make it secure. Nice walk into town. Dog friendly. There were a heaps of sites with dogs but kudos to everyone for being courteous with them.

Been staying at crusty for 3 years , great location awesome sites and always friendly staff , I see a lot of reviews with incorrect information spewing venom because they had to turn music down as it was disturbing the entire park ,damaging a business reputation out of spitefulness isn’t cool. Yes it’s not a flash park but I think that’s what I like about it , also renovations are in progress.
( Rome wasn’t built in a day people )
Cheers crusty I shall be back.
This place needs to shut down immediately the amount of disrespect and racist I saw towards a group of middle eastern campers the other day was disgusting. Ruined my whole time.
Great spot at the bottom of mystic mountain. Perfect for mountain bikers

Great little bush site for camping. We had pur camper trailer Nd the sites are big. Love the historic feel of this site and the history of it. Great access to bike rides and parasIling. A beer by the creek for an afternoon swim/splash is a must. Friendly staff with a smile who were always available. Just be prepared for busy season for the place to be pumping, however staff do their best to support the riff raff quieting doen at a reasonable time. Looking forward to coming back.

Great location. However unfortunately they seem to tolerate groups blasting their music until late at night. It is a big camp ground and there have been multiple large groups with big speaker set ups. Management does not seem to care that music is played extremely loud until after 11pm. Not sure what their policy is but people are also smoking everywhere, close to amentities and kitchen area. A better regulation around these things would make this a more family friendly place.
The workers were being rude towards us the moment we stepped onto the camp side
THE WORST SERVICE EVER DO NOT GO.

Sorry, but I really don’t recommend it because it was really dirty. The kitchen was really dirty that was the worst place I’ve ever been to like I could not even sleep at night because it was that bad the bathrooms smelled, and when I went to the toilet, and I saw a cockroach roaming around that was disgusting The service was really horrible. The girl that was Islander she called herself black. You know what she said. She said that you can’t yell at me because I’m black like you’re not black and that was so rude and when we were about to leave we packed up our stuff and she was smiling that we were gone, and when I went to my cabin, there was so much spiders and flies that were dead in the it was disgusting, so I would never recommend it

This was our second stay at Camp Crusty this year.  We love it for its location near the bike tracks and the creek, it's atmosphere (being a popular place for mountain bikers) and the friendly staff who were very helpful and accommodating during our stay.

This camp sight is crusty and rusty. they dont change the bedsheets the cabins are really dirty the  only cleaning is mopping the floor the owners are really rude nd seemed happy when we left DONT GO HERE!

Sweet place to stay for the bike park with friends. Rustic yet affordable, with basic bathrooms, bike wash, kitchen and laundry. Campsites are pretty big and it's a chill vibe from the staff, they are flexible.

For a camp that was built in the 1930's as acommodtaion for loggers anybody expecting a modern version of luxury will be disappointed. Camp Crusty is what it is. It has a laundry, shared kitchen, toilet and shower block, the bunks are comfortable and the roof doesn't leak. The staff are friendly and helpful.

The manager was extremely helpful in assisting me to book in at short notice, to help with a towel as my luggage remained locked up in my broken down car up the mountain. She also volunteered to drive me into town to catch an early morning bus. Very appreciative of all the help.
Family friendly, staff are friendly, great spot. Third time back. It’s our regular go to now

Place is a decrepit dump and a rip off for such poor quality facilities. Staff have an attitude and so you can't even say it has a good atmosphere to make up for the squalor. Needs a complete reno - surprised the council haven't shut this place down. Don't stay here.

Crusty by name, CRUSTY by nature!!

Old, dirty, run down..
Not a dollar has been spent here for 40 years by the look of this place and its facilities, I’m not looking for luxuries when I book a site, BUT when they are charging $100 for a unpowered site (4 people in two tents no cars) yep $100!!  What a joke, to top it of staff were rude and uninviting..

Save you money and camp in smoko for free and have an extra $100 for the pub!
Amazing location in amongst the trees. Could really do with upgrading the facilities though.
The Staff here are Truely Amazing they are so Welcoming, Happy , Helpful and Wonderful and place was perfect for us we will definitely be back.
Basic, but great when stuck in the rain with our dogs late evening and no one wanted to let us to stay.

very nice place
good environment
friendly people
scenery ehhmmmmeh mt buffalo

Crusty is great because they allow dogs, even over summer. I think it’s one of the only places in Bright who do.

It’s a bit of a treck into town, you’d want a bike or to drive. Sites are pretty good, if you get one out the front it’s nice to look at the hills and paraglides. But this also leaves you pretty far from the toilets. Toilets aren’t the nicest, pretty old buildings.
Stayed in cabin 9 couple hut and on arrival on Friday 29th September found floor hadn't been vacuumed or mopped. Park management seemed rude

Very basic rooms. Beds and some shelving. Camp kitchen would be terrible to use in snow or rain. Toaster, only half of one side worked. Gas bottle on BBQ ran out of gas. Toilet facilities, 2 blocks. One closed most of the times. Showers in block one has see thru curtains. NOT GOOD. There was plenty of hot water.

This is a very basic camp site. Although there appears to be some upgrades happening.

We use crusty campsite as it’s clean, in best position just out of town but in walking distance. Right next to mystic mountain, but even if this isn’t your thing, just having cows behind campsite, many tracks to walk and lots of trees and shade makes it all worthwhile.  The staff are friendly and helpful. We keep returning as just love it!
Great location and team are great to deal with. Loved my camping spot.

Tahee is the best soccer playing resident kelpie … ❤️ Great sunny campsite with plenty of space right at the mountain bike tracks. The cleanest camp sinks of any camp kitchen… and great hot showers with retro sinks 👌

Love the rustic charm of the place. All the bones are there for a great caravan park. Gorgeous countryside, great feel about it, snowy mountain country feel with gorgeous trees and mountains.
However living up to the name of Crusty. Love how it looks old world Australian pioneer country. But when you pay $57 for a powered site you expect the amenities to be top notch. Toilets and showers very old. Showers small with weak pressure. Amenities far away from camping and no bins in camping area. $30 would be a better price for what is provided and when all the upgrades are finished then charge higher.
Can only book online and then there is a booking fee.
Did have a great night's sleep here and love the feel of the place.
Great family and friends accom with everything you need and nothing you don’t!

We have now stayed at Camp Crusty a couple times as it is convenient location for the MTB Park for our kids.
The staff are friendly and happy to help us out wit a site to accommodate the caravan. We are looking forward to the many changes taking place. We will definitely be back.
Thankyou Camp Crusty.

Update since speaking with management -  Police are investigating as I believe the offender was staying in the camp behind our cabin. Management offered a different cabin but expected her to continue using the showers and bathrooms where she was filmed, so refused a refund for two nights we couldn't stay. Not concerned about the obvious safety issue that forced us to leave. Would not recommend anyone stay here.

Original review - Visited with my partner intending to stay three nights. On the second day she caught someone trying to film her in the shower. We obviously left as we didn't feel safe. I've attempted to contact the owners twice but they don't seem to care. Seemingly not concerned about this type of thing happening.
I had a powered site.   Electronic booking and communication went well.    11.00am checkout generous.
一般的露營區的小木屋～有BBQ
I've enjoyed my time here at a powered site, it is very central to so many of the things you can do in and around Bright. The managers are friendly and approachable. I would stay here again.

Beautiful location, with walking trails and a creek within the park. We stayed in a cabin with our two kelpies, and just love that the area and park is dog friendly! Everything was clean and it had everything you needed. Already planning to come back! ☺️

Love this place! I come here most weekends for the snow season and I camp (unpowered). It’s in a great spot, the grounds are always clean, it’s close to the main town, right across from a biking place and only an hour from the mountains.
I have had no issues writing any of my stays. All the staff are really friendly and accommodating, the camp kitchen has everything you need which saved me bringing more equipment with me the facilities are what you would expect from camp bathrooms. You can also hire a fire and buy wood for the colder nights which is great.
I couldn’t recommend this place more for affordable camping. I haven’t stayed in the cabins but through talking to other people staying on the grounds, I gather they are all clean and have the basic necessities.

Honestly i am not over exaggerating when i say this was one of the coziest camps I've been to. The view is not bad at all, the people are great, the rooms were cozy, just attempt to bring your own heater like i did! will be back again soon!

Leaving some honest feedback here… the photos shown when booking look nothing like the actual room you get allocated so keep your expectations low, it’s honestly surprising this can even be considered habitable accomodation in the 21st century. The bed sheets were disgusting and had stains all over them, there were no other people in sight and most of the other buildings were falling apart. We spent the whole afternoon sitting in the car hoping to find other accommodation or we were prepared to drive 5 hours back home… luckily we found somewhere else that lived up to our expectations. Definitely won’t be recommending or booking here again.

My partner and I booked for 3 nights and the first night was nice enough, basic accommodation with a camping vibe.
Unfortunately our stay was cut short as the second day I discovered someone was filming me while I was in the shower.
It is currently being investigated by police. We wouldn't stay there again, there was no security cameras anywhere on the grounds and the toilets/showers were poorly secured. Have left contact details with the park owners and still haven't heard back 24hours later.

If I could pick less then 1 star I would, we tried to stay here with our young family and to say we were horrified driving in is an understatement. I don't understand how they are allowed to remain open, surely there is a standard that must be followed. Buildings are all falling apart and looks so run down. I wouldn't let my dog sleep in them. The brand new little cabins are tiny but would be great for a couple if that's there thing.
Love this campsite. Very friendly service. Will always come back.
